text:
Let me let you guys in on a secret that can help you catch a lot of these moves before they happen so you're not stuck chasing and holding the bag.

Listen up.

Create a watchlist with the leaders in every sector that's been hot this year.

For example: Photonics, Semis, FAANG, Space, Robotics, Financials, Healthcare, Energy, etc.

Money is constantly rotating between these sectors. Your job is to determine where institutional money is positioned before each session.

Within those sector leaders, you'll have a group of smaller names that align with the same theme.

Something that really stood out to me last Friday was how memory names started bidding aggressively despite a weak tape. Those are some of my favorite setups because if the market decides to turn around, those names are usually the first to lead.

Between Thursday and Friday of last week, $SNDK ran from 1670 to 2000+.

While that name can be difficult for traders with smaller accounts to participate in, there are other stocks within the exact same theme that often make aggressive moves shortly after.

My focus immediately shifted to $WDC and $STX.

$WDC ran from 500 to 730 in just 3 sessions (+46%).

$STX ran from 870 to 1100 in just 3 sessions (+26%).

While many traders wait for 10 indicators to trigger before hitting the buy button and end up chasing these moves, there are other ways to get ahead.

The market leaves clues. You just have to know where to look.

text: Csquare's IPO filing signals a growing demand for AI-optimized infrastructure. The company aims to reshape competitive dynamics in the data center market, potentially driving innovation in AI technology. Meanwhile, Coherent Corp's $650M investment in Texas photonics could enhance U.S. semiconductor capabilities and job growth, further boosting AI optics partnerships like that with NVIDIA.

text:
AI infrastructure runs on more than compute. It runs on light.

Today in Sherman, Texas, Jensen Huang joined @CoherentCorp CEO Jim Anderson and local leaders to mark the expansion of U.S. manufacturing capacity for advanced photonics.

The technology is behind the lasers and optical links that move data across modern AI systems, strengthening the American manufacturing base behind the AI era.

text:
🚨 SCIENTISTS JUST ACHIEVED OPTICAL MULTISTABILITY IN A TINY MICROCAVITY  A BIG STEP FOR PHOTONIC MEMORY.

In a new paper published in Nature Nanotechnology, researchers demonstrated multiple stable optical states inside a compact microcavity by using near-exceptional coupling. 

This allows the device to “remember” different light intensities as distinct, stable states  something normally only possible in much larger or more complex systems.
Optical multistability is a key requirement for multilevel optical memory and all-optical computing. Until now, achieving it in a small enough footprint for on-chip use has been extremely difficult because optical nonlinearities are usually very weak.

Why this matters:

• It brings us closer to practical on-chip optical memory that can store multiple bits of information per device
• Exceptional-point physics can dramatically enhance effective nonlinearity without needing exotic materials
• Compact multistable devices could enable faster, more energy-efficient photonic processors
• It shows how non-Hermitian physics is becoming a powerful design tool in nanophotonics

The deeper implication:

We’re learning to engineer light itself to behave in increasingly complex and useful ways inside tiny structures. By operating near exceptional points, researchers can coax weak optical nonlinearities into producing strong, multistable behavior.

 This kind of advance moves photonic computing from theory toward something that can actually be fabricated at scale on a chip.

If this approach can be further optimized and integrated with existing silicon photonics, it could become a building block for future light-based memory and logic.

How do you think compact optical multistability could accelerate the development of photonic computers?

Follow for more frontier nanophotonics, exceptional-point physics, and optical computing.

Nokia in Allentown, PA — 10x capacity for photonics packaging.  

Coherent in Sherman, TX — adding capacity for InP compound semi, this is a 6" line.
Copper is dying. Photonics is almost ready. Almost has been a great year for photonics. Every year.

Hottest Sectors off the June 5th bloodbath 🩸

I know it's just been a few days but never too early to see which sectors are jumping out.

So here's the list. 7 trading days data only.

+23% Memory/Storage $SNDK $MU
+20% Payments $DAVE $SEZL
+14%  Neo-clouds $NBIS $IREN $CRWV
+14%    Networking $ALAB $CRDO
+13%  My Current Portfolio
+8%    Semis $SMH
+7%    My Older Portfolio from March 30 lows
+4%    Energy/Power: $BE $GEV $VRT
+2%    S&P 500 $SPX
0%      Photonics/Optics $LITE $AAOI $AXTI
0%      Compute $NVDA $AVGO
-3%     Software $APP $RDDT
-9%    Defense $ONDS $KRKNF

So what's the insight?

Memory, Payments, Networking and Noe-clouds off to a blazing start. Payments is new in this move up. Early signs these could be the leaders of the next leg up.

I am happy with the portfolio re-shuffle. As you can see, my current portfolio is already pulling away from the one I held during the initial push off from March 30 lows. 

My bet is power and photonics will catch up in the next market grind higher. All these stocks have been taking a breather.

The bottleneck already moved. A GPU that computes in nanoseconds and waits microseconds for data is a stranded asset. At 1.6T speeds, copper runs out of physics. The constraint on AI is no longer how fast you can think. It's how fast you can move what you thought. Jensen has now said it twice in three months. At GTC in March: "Is copper going to still be important? The answer is yes... Are you going to scale up optical? Yes. Are you going to scale out optical? Yes... We need a lot more capacity for copper. We need a lot more capacity for optics. We need a lot more capacity for CPO." Last week at Computex, on Marvell's stage: "Optics where you must, copper where you can." Then he called Marvell the next trillion-dollar company and the optical complex repriced within days. The same keynote put a date on the handoff: 200G per lane is the last generation where copper is sufficient. After that, optics takes the rack. Translation: not copper OR light. Copper now, light next, unprecedented amounts of both. 🔥 The chain is unavoidable: AI tokens are profitable → more GPUs → more bandwidth → copper hits its wall → photonics becomes the chokepoint. And the smart money stopped debating. Follow the closed deals: → $NVDA has committed at least $6.5B to photonics in three months: $2B into Lumentum, $2B into Coherent, a $500M stake in Corning, and a piece of Ayar Labs' $500M round. Direct investments to secure its own light supply. → $MRVL paid $3.25B for Celestial AI, up to $5.5B with milestones, to build what its CEO calls a silicon photonics powerhouse. → $CRDO closed DustPhotonics two weeks ago. Ciena bought CPO startup Nubis for $270M. North of $10B of strategic capital locked up one supply chain in under a year. Capital like that doesn't chase a theme. It secures a bottleneck. LAYER 1: WAFER. Every laser starts as a crystal. 🟠 $AXTI: the InP substrate leader. The first chokepoint in the stack. 🟡 $IQE: compound-semi epiwafers feeding the laser makers. Speculative, but structurally upstream. LAYER 2: LIGHT. Photons don't make themselves. 🟠 $LITE: revenue +90% YoY last quarter to $808M. EML shipments doubled and management says demand still exceeds supply across EMLs, pump lasers, and transceivers. NVIDIA just wired them $2B. OCS backlog past $400M plus a multi-hundred-million CPO order for 2027. 🟢 $SIVEF (Stockholm: SIVE): the external light source. CPO does not emit its own light. Every optical engine needs a continuous-wave InP laser feeding it, and that is the layer you cannot engineer around. ELS modules with POET hit production readiness end of this year. Disclosure: long. 🟣 $POET: the optical engine wildcard. Its Optical Interposer pairs with Sivers' lasers on external light sources for CPO, with a LITEON module deal stacked on top. Binary commercialization, real architecture. LAYER 3: OPTICS AND MODULES. Where light meets the rack. 🟠 $COHR: the volume anchor in transceivers, holding NVIDIA's other $2B check. 🔵 $AAOI: Q1 revenue +51% to a record $151M, datacenter revenue more than doubled, $124M of 800G orders plus a $200M+ 1.6T order in hand. Scaling Texas capacity toward 500K+ units a month by year-end, targeting $1B+ revenue this year. Domestic supply while everyone fights over offshore. Disclosure: long. 🟠 $FN: the foundry of optics. When Fabrinet is building, the orders already exist. THE INTERCONNECT: the layer the rack cannot route around. 🔵 $CRDO: just closed DustPhotonics. SerDes → DSP → silicon photonics → system integration, one company, 800G through 3.2T. Electrical AND optical, end to end. FY26 revenue tripled to $1.34B at 68% gross margin. The toll booth on both roads. Disclosure: long. 🟠 $MRVL: $3.25B for Celestial AI, and Jensen's trillion-dollar nod on the Computex stage. 🟠 $AVGO: switch silicon, optical DSPs, CPO engines. They define the socket. 🟠 $ANET: the AI spine. 100K-GPU clusters get stitched together in light. LAYER 4: PACKAGING, FIBER, FOUNDRY. Where photons get industrialized. 🔵 $TSEM: the neutral silicon photonics foundry. Prints wafers for whoever wins. 🟣 $LPKF: glass-substrate packaging for glass-based CPO. Real technology, binary commercialization. 🟠 $GLW: AI racks demand several times the fiber density of legacy cloud, and NVIDIA just took a $500M stake. Corning sells density. LAYER 5: TEST AND THE ANALOG UNDERLAYER. Complexity is a tax paid in validation. 🔵 $AEHR: silicon photonics test, ramping with the cycle. ' 🔵 $VIAV: every 800G and 1.6T transceiver gets validated before it ships. The gate the market prices like an accessory. 🔵 $SMTC: the drivers and TIAs that fire the lasers. Sits directly under the LPO trade. 🔵 $MTSI: the high-speed analog behind 1.6T engines. 🟠 $CIEN: transport. Even long-haul is buying light. 💡The counter-thesis, because every map needs one. The honest debate on this stack is whether these are genuine bottleneck assets or cyclical optics suppliers enjoying peak demand at peak multiples. Lumentum's May print showed +90% growth with the stock up roughly 1,400% over the prior year at a triple-digit trailing multiple. That is a price for perfection. Most of these names live or die on a handful of hyperscaler capex lines, and one digestion quarter hits the whole stack at once. CPO timing has already slipped once. Architecture risk is real: LPO, CPO, and stretched copper are still fighting for the same sockets. The cycle is real. So is the gravity. 🔥 But the bears have to explain one thing: $NVDA, $MRVL, $CRDO, and $CIEN just spent over $10B securing this supply chain with their own balance sheets. The people with the best information are paying up for the layers. The market owns the top of this stack. The asymmetry is at the edges: wafer, light, packaging, test. Own the layers, not the logo. Bookmark this for the weekend. Then tag the one investor you know who's still all compute and no interconnect. 👀"}}}, "rest_id": "2065518398872961392", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "100578", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2066309157569851897", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "98", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: I'm old enough to remember when buying $RIVN was going to be the next big thing.  Which of the photonics stocks is the next Rivian or are they all winners? https://t.co/P02o4huns4

FÁBRICAS EN EL ESPACIO

$SPCX 🚀

En un futuro, con costos de transporte espacial bajos, la fabricación y servicios en órbita se vuelven viables y rentables, especialmente para productos de alto valor donde la ausencia de gravedad mejora significativamente la calidad, reduce defectos por convección/sedimentación o permite estructuras imposibles en Tierra.

Fabricación de Materiales Avanzados

🔸Fibras ópticas ZBLAN (o similares): En microgravedad se producen fibras con mucho menos defectos cristalinos y pérdidas de señal (hasta 10-100 veces mejores que en Tierra). Ideales para telecomunicaciones de alta velocidad, láseres médicos y sensores. Empresas como Flawless Photonics, FOMS y Made In Space ya han demostrado producción en la ISS (kilómetros de fibra).

🔸Cristales de proteínas y farmacéuticos: Crecimiento de cristales más grandes, uniformes y puros para drogas (ej. ritonavir, anticuerpos monoclonales). Mejora la formulación de medicamentos, permite nuevas terapias y acelera el descubrimiento. Varda Space ya retornó productos fabricados en órbita.

🔸Semiconductores y chips: Fabricación con menor contaminación por gravedad, mejor uniformidad en dopaje y cristales. Potencial para superar limitaciones terrestres en rendimiento y escalabilidad (apoyado por CHIPS Act).

🔸Aleaciones y materiales compuestos metálicos: Mezclas más homogéneas sin sedimentación (ej. espumas metálicas, superaleaciones). Mejores propiedades estructurales para aeronáutica, energía y herramientas.

🔸Nanomateriales y vidrios especializados: Producción de nanomateriales, vidrios exóticos o películas delgadas con propiedades únicas gracias al vacío y ausencia de convección.

🔸Impresión 3D y fabricación aditiva en órbita: Partes complejas sin soportes, estructuras grandes (vigas, paneles) optimizadas para espacio. Reduce masa lanzada y permite reparaciones in-situ o construcción de hábitats/telescopios.

🔸Bioprinting y tejidos/regenerativa: Impresión de órganos, retinas artificiales o tejidos en 3D sin colapso por gravedad. LambdaVision y otros trabajan en membranas para restaurar visión.

Servicios y Otras Aplicaciones

🔹Ensamblaje y mantenimiento de grandes estructuras: Construcción de estaciones espaciales, paneles solares orbitales, telescopios gigantes o estaciones de energía solar (Space-Based Solar Power). Más fácil manejar componentes masivos sin peso.

🔹Depósitos de combustible y logística orbital: Fabricación/refinamiento de propelentes o estaciones de servicio para naves (refueling). Clave para misiones profundas.

🔹Investigación y testing de materiales: Laboratorios permanentes para probar comportamientos en microgravedad (fluidos, combustión, biología) que aceleran innovación terrestre.

🔹Producción de productos biológicos y dispositivos médicos: Cultivo de células, modelado de enfermedades o dispositivos implantables con mejor control de fluidos.

🔹Turismo y experiencias en microgravedad: fábricas orbitales podrían ofrecer tours o entornos controlados para entrenamiento/research.

🔹Reciclaje y economía circular orbital: Reprocesar desechos espaciales o satélites en nuevos materiales, reduciendo basura orbital y costos.

Consideraciones Generales

☑️Ventajas clave: Ausencia de convección/burbujas/sedimentación, vacío natural, energía solar abundante y disipación de calor fácil. Ideal para productos de alto valor por kg (fibras ópticas o fármacos justifican fácilmente el transporte).

☑️Desafíos actuales: Retorno seguro a Tierra, automatización/robótica (para reducir tripulación), regulaciones (FDA para fármacos) y escalabilidad. Con costos bajos, se abre un mercado comercial masivo.

☑️Empresas pioneras: Axiom Space, Redwire/Made In Space, Varda, Flawless Photonics, Sierra Space, etc.

Esta lista se basa en avances reales y proyecciones hacia 2030+. Con costos por kg cayendo drásticamente, pasará de nicho a industria transformadora, similar a cómo la reutilización revolucionó los lanzamientos.

text:
$XFAB - The next $TSEM for Europe?

Largest pure-play SiC foundry globally being the only high-volume manufacturing capacity in the US.
 
6 fabs worldwide, and over 400 customers. (#1 foundry supplier for 12 of the top 20)

Sounds incredibly strong at first. But don't be fooled. We are going deeper.

Today, it looks clear that two cycles could be the major upside for this company: power semiconductors and silicon photonics.

1. Power Semis shows direct exposure to $NVDA 800 VDC: $NVTS (heavily relies on them for SiC), and also partners with $POWI and Lite-On.

2. Photonics exposure is through photonixFAB consortium, and three ongoing major projects in development (high-volume production in 2028). $NOK and $NVDA are active evaluators (not confirmed customers yet).

There is also explicit backing through the US and European CHIPS Acts which validates their strategic importance for the West. 

Their tech covers a wide spectrum of CMOS, SOI, SiC, GaN, MEMs, and MTP. They operate on 200 mm wafers and node ranging from 110nm-350nm. They don't compete with customers on IC design, assembly, test or IC sales - they are a pure-play foundry. Partnerships include $IQE, $SOI, Cadence. 

Financially, it has delivered 17% CAGR over the last 5 years. Recent Q1 numbers show revenue growth was down 2%, and that the business is split into automotive (down), industrial (up 32% YoY) and medical (up). Balance sheet is quite stable with $145M in cash and $291M in net debt. 

Melexis (automotive exposure customer) accounts for ~40% of their revenue. Strong baseline but also a concentration factor.

Now here goes comes MY take and view. 

> Technology gap compared to $TSEM: For power semis, their 200mm wafer platform is excellent for high-volume manufacturing. However, for the advanced silicon photonics cycle, tier-1 players prefer migrating toward 300mm wafers to lower optical losses and shrink component footprints. This shows X-FAB is still not the absolute best or preferred platform for the premium photonics cycle yet.

> Growth upside looks heavily weighted towards photonics. Power semis will add solid revenue, but it won't be as explosive as photonics. One reason is because other IDMs like $IFX, $STM, $ON, $WOLF own their own internal fabs and don't rely on X-FAB. Shows they are not the only one in the game or as significant in town for the power semi cycle. We also don't have a lot more details on their three main ongoing photonic projects. So it is hard to be extremely bullish upfront.

> 2026 is going to be a year of steady recovery, and until mid-2027 it doesn't look like the company will show significant revenue growth. Recent Q1 call also looked more bearish than bullish, and it confirmed these timelines. Plenty of time to accumulate shares for those who wish to. Would I suggest that though? Keep reading.

> The downside risk is still really low because Melexis (major revenue stream) is guiding sales growth for H2'26, and with $XFAB at ~1x sales and $1B market cap, the upside is way more here. Seriously way more.

Ultimately, this stock has immense potential. Would definitely be my asymmetrical pick over others that have already run so much like $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, etc. 

It looks like a 1 to 2 year long-term play, and not a quick few-months gain stock. If timelines compress, execution shows better than expected and they continue to improve on their technology sooner, we could easily see the narrative shift rapidly. This looks like something which I am optimistic on happening because that's how quick the AI infrastructure market is going forward overall. Every few months, it just surprises us.

I feel $XFAB can really go 10x or even more in the next few years, but this company needs to execute really well. It still hasn't earned the "next $TSEM" tag, as they aren't worthy of it today.

How would I play this?

- Very worth buying a decent amount of shares here, and holding for the next 2 years. Mainly because upside is 10x+ if things fall in place.

- But I wouldn't go all in at once. Rather build my position slowly and continue to accumulate over time as the story plays out.

NFA. DYOR.

Largest pure-play SiC foundry globally being the only high-volume manufacturing capacity in the US. 6 fabs worldwide, and over 400 customers. (#1 foundry supplier for 12 of the top 20) Sounds incredibly strong at first. But don't be fooled. We are going deeper. Today, it looks clear that two cycles could be the major upside for this company: power semiconductors and silicon photonics. 1. Power Semis shows direct exposure to $NVDA 800 VDC: $NVTS (heavily relies on them for SiC), and also partners with $POWI and Lite-On. 2. Photonics exposure is through photonixFAB consortium, and three ongoing major projects in development (high-volume production in 2028). $NOK and $NVDA are active evaluators (not confirmed customers yet). There is also explicit backing through the US and European CHIPS Acts which validates their strategic importance for the West. Their tech covers a wide spectrum of CMOS, SOI, SiC, GaN, MEMs, and MTP. They operate on 200 mm wafers and node ranging from 110nm-350nm. They don't compete with customers on IC design, assembly, test or IC sales - they are a pure-play foundry. Partnerships include $IQE, $SOI, Cadence. Financially, it has delivered 17% CAGR over the last 5 years. Recent Q1 numbers show revenue growth was down 2%, and that the business is split into automotive (down), industrial (up 32% YoY) and medical (up). Balance sheet is quite stable with $145M in cash and $291M in net debt. Melexis (automotive exposure customer) accounts for ~40% of their revenue. Strong baseline but also a concentration factor. Now here goes comes MY take and view. > Technology gap compared to $TSEM: For power semis, their 200mm wafer platform is excellent for high-volume manufacturing. However, for the advanced silicon photonics cycle, tier-1 players prefer migrating toward 300mm wafers to lower optical losses and shrink component footprints. This shows X-FAB is still not the absolute best or preferred platform for the premium photonics cycle yet. > Growth upside looks heavily weighted towards photonics. Power semis will add solid revenue, but it won't be as explosive as photonics. One reason is because other IDMs like $IFX, $STM, $ON, $WOLF own their own internal fabs and don't rely on X-FAB. Shows they are not the only one in the game or as significant in town for the power semi cycle. We also don't have a lot more details on their three main ongoing photonic projects. So it is hard to be extremely bullish upfront. > 2026 is going to be a year of steady recovery, and until mid-2027 it doesn't look like the company will show significant revenue growth. Recent Q1 call also looked more bearish than bullish, and it confirmed these timelines. Plenty of time to accumulate shares for those who wish to. Would I suggest that though? Keep reading. > The downside risk is still really low because Melexis (major revenue stream) is guiding sales growth for H2'26, and with $XFAB at ~1x sales and $1B market cap, the upside is way more here. Seriously way more. Ultimately, this stock has immense potential. Would definitely be my asymmetrical pick over others that have already run so much like $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, etc. It looks like a 1 to 2 year long-term play, and not a quick few-months gain stock. If timelines compress, execution shows better than expected and they continue to improve on their technology sooner, we could easily see the narrative shift rapidly. This looks like something which I am optimistic on happening because that's how quick the AI infrastructure market is going forward overall. Every few months, it just surprises us. I feel $XFAB can really go 10x or even more in the next few years, but this company needs to execute really well. It still hasn't earned the "next $TSEM" tag, as they aren't worthy of it today. How would I play this? - Very worth buying a decent amount of shares here, and holding for the next 2 years. Mainly because upside is 10x+ if things fall in place. - But I wouldn't go all in at once. Rather build my position slowly and continue to accumulate over time as the story plays out. NFA. DYOR."}}}, "rest_id": "2066281077564383511", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "31038", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$AAOI seeing heavy upside call flow

Largest order was the 177.5C block (~$611K), with additional buying in 200C/210C/280C/300C strikes

One put sweep at 150P showed up, but overall this is a clear bullish continuation bet

Recent catalyst mix includes AI/photonics momentum, analyst upgrades, and earnings-driven attention

text:
My core holdings right now:

AI infra / neocloud — $NBIS, IREN, NVDA
Fintech / software — HOOD, NOW, CRWD, RDDT
Memory — MU, DRAM
AI power / grid — VST, CEG, VRT, FLNC
Photonics / optical — MRVL, LITE, GLW, COHR
Metals hedge — GLD, SLV calls

Changes since last portfolio post:
Added GLD/SLV call basket.
Added/pressed some NVDA, NBIS, META, NOW, AMZN/VRT call exposure.
Trimmed IREN.
SG is out.
SOXL/SOXS are not core — just tactical trading junk, not a thesis.

Most of the real research here is still around AI infra bottlenecks: neocloud power, memory, photonics, and grid capacity.

But the actual book is broader than this account makes it look: software/fintech and a metals hedge matter too.

The honest state: winners are working, but options/cash leverage is high enough that I'm not trying to broad-add into green tape. New money has to earn it with proof or a pullback.

We'll see which ones earn their way in

text:
“Technical Analysis is Trader’s Astrology” 

Serenity’s Sharp Critique on What Really Moves Markets "White-Haired Stock God" Serenity has sparked debate with a bold take: Technical Analysis (TA) functions more like astrology for traders than a reliable predictive tool. She argues it’s primarily confirmation bias combined with crowd psychology, useful for gauging sentiment and timing entries but not the core driver of prices. Her Key Points: Massive stock surges are rarely caused by chart patterns (golden crosses, dragon candles, etc.).
They are driven by fundamentals and forward expectations. $SIVE +1900%: Market repricing future revenue from $JBL and $GFS catalysts.
$AXTI +8000%: Industry logic including indium phosphide substrates, photonics demand, export controls, and ASP dynamics.

Serenity’s Advice:
TA can help identify psychological entry points, but sustainable price trends are determined by: Industry theme linkages
Earnings expectation changes
Macro environment
Financial report quality
Circulating share structure (float)

Long-term stock potential should be judged by fundamentals and capital structure, not “chart faith. ”Why This Resonates:
In a market full of noise — especially post major IPOs like SpaceX — focusing on real catalysts (revenue growth, competitive advantages, macro tailwinds) provides a clearer edge than pattern chasing.

Investment Takeaway:
This is a timely reminder for traders and investors alike. While charts can be entertaining and occasionally helpful for short-term psychology, the biggest winners come from understanding the underlying business story and capital flows. Bottom Line: Serenity urges a return to basics. In the long run, fundamentals and capital structure trump technical patterns. A healthy dose of skepticism toward “chart magic” can protect capital and improve decision-making. Strong words from a respected voice in the community. 

Do you agree that TA is mostly astrology, or does it still play a key role in your strategy? Drop your thoughts below!

text:
Cheaper tokens ≠ less compute.  
They trigger more compute demand.  

This is Jevons Paradox in AI: cheaper intelligence causes total consumption to explode as new use cases, agentic workflows, and broader adoption become viable.  

Model giants like OpenAI face real price-war pressure. But the picks-and-shovels layer (power, optics, platforms) wins on volume. $IREN , $AAOI , $PLTR and many more are set up beautifully.  

Core insight from recent discussions (Nebius/20VC with Roman Chernin, BG2 Pod, Jordi Visser): every time cost-per-token or cost-per-unit intelligence drops, demand rises, not because people use less, but because they use far more. Previously uneconomic tasks become viable, complex multi-step agentic workflows scale, and new markets open.  

Nebius saw some of its best sales weeks right after major cheap open-source model drops. The market isn’t near its ceiling.  

Jordi Visser laid it out clearly: price compression and commoditization at the model layer (OpenAI/Anthropic facing drastic cuts, 80% of workloads shifting to far cheaper models) versus real inflation and bottlenecks at the physical layer (power, materials, networking, optics). Demand for intelligence is near-infinite. Cheaper per-token pricing unlocks the floodgates on total tokens consumed.  

Who benefits? Model-layer players can get squeezed on margins even as headline revenue grows. The infrastructure and enablement layer feasts on the volume explosion:  

• $IREN — Sustainable power, land, and AI/HPC cloud pivot. Power is the ultimate bottleneck.
• $AAOI — Optical transceivers and photonics for AI data centers. Hyperscalers are ordering 800G+ modules in volume as faster, power-efficient connectivity becomes non-negotiable.  
• $PLTR — Enterprise AI platforms (AIP/Gotham). As agentic workflows and production deployments explode, companies need real software to deploy, govern, and scale AI not just raw GPUs.  

Many more names across power, cooling, networking, and specialized infra will ride the same wave.  

@BG2Pod highlighted the bullish math: monetization per gigawatt is rising, not falling. Demand outstrips supply. Inference revenue is already running well over $200B annualized and climbing fast. Agents will multiply token consumption further. Cheaper tokens plus efficiency gains expand what’s possible within power envelopes, driving more overall buildout.  

This is why the “AI mid-cycle slowdown” narrative is incomplete. It’s healthy digestion and rotation inside a secular bull market. Capital is rotating toward the real beneficiaries of the physical buildout and the software layer that turns raw compute into usable intelligence at scale.  

The compute market remains early. We’re still in the first innings of widespread agentic adoption and enterprise transformation.  

Cheaper intelligence democratizes access → more users and businesses adopt → agents multiply usage per task → total token/compute demand detonates. Model giants feel price pressure. Infra and platform winners feel the volume tailwind.

They trigger more compute demand. This is Jevons Paradox in AI: cheaper intelligence causes total consumption to explode as new use cases, agentic workflows, and broader adoption become viable. Model giants like OpenAI face real price-war pressure. But the picks-and-shovels layer (power, optics, platforms) wins on volume. $IREN , $AAOI , $PLTR and many more are set up beautifully. Core insight from recent discussions (Nebius/20VC with Roman Chernin, BG2 Pod, Jordi Visser): every time cost-per-token or cost-per-unit intelligence drops, demand rises, not because people use less, but because they use far more. Previously uneconomic tasks become viable, complex multi-step agentic workflows scale, and new markets open. Nebius saw some of its best sales weeks right after major cheap open-source model drops. The market isn’t near its ceiling. Jordi Visser laid it out clearly: price compression and commoditization at the model layer (OpenAI/Anthropic facing drastic cuts, 80% of workloads shifting to far cheaper models) versus real inflation and bottlenecks at the physical layer (power, materials, networking, optics). Demand for intelligence is near-infinite. Cheaper per-token pricing unlocks the floodgates on total tokens consumed. Who benefits? Model-layer players can get squeezed on margins even as headline revenue grows. The infrastructure and enablement layer feasts on the volume explosion: • $IREN — Sustainable power, land, and AI/HPC cloud pivot. Power is the ultimate bottleneck. • $AAOI — Optical transceivers and photonics for AI data centers. Hyperscalers are ordering 800G+ modules in volume as faster, power-efficient connectivity becomes non-negotiable. • $PLTR — Enterprise AI platforms (AIP/Gotham). As agentic workflows and production deployments explode, companies need real software to deploy, govern, and scale AI not just raw GPUs. Many more names across power, cooling, networking, and specialized infra will ride the same wave. @BG2Pod highlighted the bullish math: monetization per gigawatt is rising, not falling. Demand outstrips supply. Inference revenue is already running well over $200B annualized and climbing fast. Agents will multiply token consumption further. Cheaper tokens plus efficiency gains expand what’s possible within power envelopes, driving more overall buildout. This is why the “AI mid-cycle slowdown” narrative is incomplete. It’s healthy digestion and rotation inside a secular bull market. Capital is rotating toward the real beneficiaries of the physical buildout and the software layer that turns raw compute into usable intelligence at scale. The compute market remains early. We’re still in the first innings of widespread agentic adoption and enterprise transformation. Cheaper intelligence democratizes access → more users and businesses adopt → agents multiply usage per task → total token/compute demand detonates. Model giants feel price pressure. Infra and platform winners feel the volume tailwind."}}}, "rest_id": "2066252039068262859", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "337", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🤖 US startup starts work on ‘first utility-scale quantum computer’ in Australia

American startup PsiQuantum has begun construction on a facility in Moreton Bay, Australia, where it plans to build and deploy what it calls the world’s first utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer.

Unlike many quantum computing efforts that remain focused on laboratory-scale systems, PsiQuantum is pursuing a photonics-based approach designed to scale using existing semiconductor manufacturing processes and optical networking technologies.

“Building a quantum computer that solves real world problems is one of the great engineering challenges of our time. For decades, quantum computing has held the promise of transforming what humanity can achieve through computation, and today in Australia we are beginning to turn that promise into reality,” — said Victor Peng, CEO of PsiQuantum.

The company believes fault-tolerant quantum computers could eventually tackle problems beyond the reach of conventional computing systems. Potential applications include drug discovery, materials development, energy research, logistics, manufacturing, agriculture, and financial modeling.

For decades, quantum computing has held the promise of transforming what humanity can achieve through computation, and today in Australia we are beginning to turn that promise into reality,” — said Victor Peng, CEO of PsiQuantum. The company believes fault-tolerant quantum computers could eventually tackle problems beyond the reach of conventional computing systems. text:
$MRVL just detailed a major leap in silicon photonics for the AI era. 🔥

Conventional photonic modulators are hitting the diffraction limit of light—they can’t scale fast enough for the insane bandwidth AI clusters will demand.

Plasmonics changes the game. By squeezing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, Marvell’s team is unlocking speeds >1 THz (10x+ faster than today’s best photonic modulators), devices only ~10 microns long (300–500x smaller), and exponentially higher bandwidth density all inside the existing silicon photonics platform.

No need for exotic new materials. Just smarter physics.

This directly powers the optical interconnects for next-gen scale-up and scale-across AI networks, exactly where Marvell has been building leadership (custom silicon + high-speed optics). They’ve already delivered high-speed SiPho devices with plasmonic tech in 2026, with the roadmap pointing to 3.2T+ modules and big wins in power and space efficiency.

This foundational tech compounds over 5–10 years as AI infrastructure scales. Long-term holders know: connectivity is becoming as critical as the accelerators themselves.

text:
$ALMU - THE PUBLIC PROXY FOR PHOTONICS YOU CAN'T BUY IN ANDURIL OR LIGHTMATTER, STILL PRE-REVENUE

Aeluma rose ~6% to $25 as the AI-optics complex snapped back from yesterday's rate flush. Its pitch: manufacturing III-V compound semiconductors — the lasers and detectors photonics needs — directly on 300mm silicon wafers with CMOS-compatible processes, partnered with Tower Semiconductor and Sumitomo, with quantum-dot lasers aimed at data-center co-packaged optics.

Be honest about the stage: fiscal-2026 revenue is guided to just $4.2–4.6M from R&D contracts, not production hardware, with no named commercial design win yet. This is a call option on an inevitability — the bet that scalable III-V-on-silicon is the missing manufacturing layer when CPO goes volume — not a revenue story today.

text:
$AEHR - THE BURN-IN BOTTLENECK NOBODY PRICES UNTIL AI CHIPS START FAILING IN THE FIELD

Aehr Test Systems rose ~7% to $113 as investors re-rated its wafer-level burn-in franchise into a William Blair presentation and a fresh June deck mapping AI-processor and data-center demand. The company already booked a record $41M production order from a leading hyperscale AI customer this spring and is now chasing a development deal for HBM4E memory-optimized blades, targeting fiscal-2027 orders.

The mechanism is reliability at scale: as SiC, GaN, photonics and HBM go into multi-thousand-GPU racks, every die has to be stressed before it ships — and Aehr is the lone pure-play in wafer-level burn-in across all four, the second derivative of the AI buildout.

text:
$NOK

Nokia is a screaming buy right now

The company has unveiled plans to expand its photonic chip facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania with the additional capacity expected to be operational by the end of Q3 2026

The fact that you can still buy this stock at $13 tells me the market still treats this company as a mobile phone business 

Nokia has taken control of one of the most significant photonics manufacturing facilities in the United States and is now driving a tenfold scale up that is backed by government funding

This stock should easily be $30

text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 17, 2026

📊 SMH: $623.97 (+1.29%)

📈 Earnings:
• JBL — $8.8B rev (est $8.66B), $3.16 core EPS. Core operating margin 5.7%, +12% YoY. Raised FY26 outlook to $35B revenue, 5.8% core operating margin, $12.70 core EPS, and $1.4B+ adjusted FCF as AI infrastructure demand stayed extremely strong. Stock finished +1.9%.

🔥 Major Developments:
• AEHR received another follow-on production order for a fully automated FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in system from a major silicon photonics customer serving hyperscale data-center optical interconnects, with additional systems forecast this year as next-gen capacity ramps.
• Intel’s 18A-P process entered risk production, moving its most advanced foundry node closer to external customer qualification and keeping the Apple/advanced-node second-source debate alive.
• NVIDIA Blackwell added day-zero support for MiniMax M3, a 428B-parameter MoE model with 1M-token context, reinforcing the push toward high-throughput agentic inference on Blackwell systems.
• AI infrastructure breadth kept widening: Jabil raised its FY26 outlook on hyperscale demand, while Aehr’s optical burn-in order points to production bottlenecks moving deeper into photonics reliability, test, and manufacturing capacity.

From raw materials to finished systems, here’s how the entire photonics stack is positioned to benefit from exploding demand in AI data centers, optical networking, and high-speed computing.

Layer 1 - Materials & Wafers
 $GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS

Layer 2 - Core Photonic Devices
 $IPGP $COHR $LITE $LASR $SIVE

Layer 3 - Components & Modules
 $AAOI $MTSI $FN $VIAV $LPTH

Layer 4 - Systems & Equipment
 $ASML $BESI $ASM $LPKF $MKS

Layer 5 - Test, Metrology & Yield
 $CAMT $FORM $AEHR $ONTO $VIAV 

Photonics is critical for moving massive amounts of data at the speed of light, exactly what hyperscalers need as AI training scales. 

Many of these names are flying under the radar but sit at the heart of the next leg of AI infrastructure spend.

text:
Very strong results from $JBL in Q3 FY2026

They reported $8.8B in revenue and core EPS of $3.16

Beating market expectations and raising guidance for the fourth quarter

The most relevant part is that they highlighted strong demand in AI infrastructure

Which led them to increase their annual revenue projection for this segment to $13.6B

This is excellent news for the entire supply chain

Especially for companies like $SIVE and $AAOI 

IMO these numbers act as a positive catalyst for $SIVE within a clearly bullish environment for the photonics sector and hyperscalers capex

Very bullish on $SIVE anda $AAOI

text:
I’ve been saying for a while that energy is the next bottleneck after memory/photonics. 

The companies that are able to provide and lend the energy will see MASSIVE returns over the coming years. 

Energy is going to be the most critical component of advancing our infrastructure, whether it is for AI or not. 

Now $NVDA is telling you that energy is more critical than you can imagine. What will you do about it anon?

$SLNH $SMR $ENPH $TE $NUAI

text:
The generalists completely misjudged $TRT looking at it through the lens of a legacy semiconductor testing company.

They missed the absolute mechanical reality that you cannot scale high-performance computing without cross-stack reliability testing, and the data layer shows all four pillars are now fully running.
The thesis hitting the tape proves exactly why the runway toward a $1B valuation has so much structural momentum:

Memory & Compute Convergence: Their back-end semiconductor solutions segment just hit a massive 124% YoY explosion. Securing multi-million dollar follow-on orders for specialized burn-in boards backstopping next-generation AI GPU platforms proves they are an un-bypassable gatekeeper for the core compute and HBM stack.  

The Power & Photonics Layer: The crowd is totally blind to their dynamic tester system shipments for Silicon Carbide (SiC) and Gallium Nitride (GaN) power modules. As advanced hardware environments transition from copper to light, the structural heat and signal redlining mean automated power testing and optical co-packaging reliability are mandatory toll-booths, not optional choices.  

They are aggressively expanding their physical footprint with a massive new 104,000-square-foot facility in Malaysia just to absorb the sheer velocity of AI hardware testing demand.  

The tourists are still trying to figure out if the momentum is real, while the smart money is just watching the fundamental checklist get ticked off one by one. Still incredibly early in the infrastructure cycle.  

Long the cross-stack bottlenecks.

text:
The market cap disparity here is where the absolute percentage torque is hiding.

While generalists chase the massive liquidity pool of a larger cap play like $AAOI, they completely miss the structural math of relative scaling. Owning both is the ultimate play on the broader optical super-cycle, but ignoring the sheer headroom on Sivers ($SIVE) at these valuation levels is a massive blind spot.

The structural reality between the two tells you exactly where the asymmetric upside sits:

The Valuation Ceiling: $AAOI is an exceptional, vertically integrated photonics asset, but a larger market cap means moving the needle 2x or 3x takes an astronomical amount of capital. $SIVE sitting at a fraction of that size means any major US hyperscaler contract win translates into immediate, hyper-violent torque on the stock price.

The Transatlantic Re-Rate: The market continues to discount Sivers because of its European listing heritage, failing to see the structural pipeline of its ongoing US flip. As it completes the transition into a fully US-domiciled entity, it systematically opens the floodgates for domestic small-cap growth funds that are contractually prohibited from buying foreign listings.
 
The 100 SEK Threshold: Complaining about a past 2,000% run is classic rear-view mirror trading. Crossing the 100 SEK ($10) mark didn't signal the end of the move, it cleared the institutional liquidity mandates that kept the largest desks from accumulating the float.

You don't have to pick a single winner when the entire copper-to-light paradigm shift is choking out global capacity. But if you are hunting for raw, high-torque percentage expansion as the independent Western chokepoint gets priced in, the path of least resistance is incredibly clear.

Long both, but $AAOI is my personal favorite ! 📈

Traditional electronics move data using electricity through copper wires. Photonics moves data using light through fiber-optic cables. Light can carry much more information and travel much farther than electrical signals. ⸻ What is a transceiver? A transceiver is a small device that sits at each end of a fiber-optic cable. Its job is to: Take electrical data from a server or switch. -Convert it into flashes of light. -Send that light through fiber. -Receive light coming back. -Convert it back into electrical data. Think of it as a translator between electronics and light. ⸻ Where does $AAOI fit in? $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) manufactures: -Optical transceivers -Lasers -Fiber-optic networking components Their products are used inside: AI data centers Cloud providers Telecom networks Broadband networks ⸻ Why AI is making this important A modern AI data center may contain: Thousands of GPUs Massive amounts of data moving between them The GPUs can’t work together efficiently unless they can communicate extremely fast. That’s where optical networking comes in. The data path looks like: GPU → Switch → Transceiver → Fiber → Transceiver → Switch → GPU Every connection requires optical hardware."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2067444958332584107", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "36729", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2068110479390855576", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "9776", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 19, 2026

📊 SMH: $659.88 (+5.76%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• China’s AI-infrastructure roadmap keeps broadening: a proposed ~$295B five-year data-center buildout is pulling domestic optics, InP substrates, EMLs, CW lasers, silicon photonics, and optical engines deeper into the strategic supply chain.
• Rubin Ultra’s memory stack debate shifted toward 12-hi HBM near term rather than 16-hi, with hybrid-bonding cost/yield still looking too painful for broad HBM insertion before later generations.
• Architect Labs raised $24M for custom chip development, another sign that venture dollars are still hunting for AI silicon alternatives beyond the GPU incumbents.
• John Jumper, the Nobel-winning AlphaFold leader, left Google DeepMind for Anthropic, extending the frontier-lab talent war that ultimately feeds the compute, networking, and power arms race.

text:
Regarding $SIVE vs $AAOI

$AAOI is in photonics and thus well positioned, but it has baggage in its history and 13b market cap.

$SIVEV is in photonics and has a clean history and a 2.8b market cap.

It's easy to see Sivers stands to gain a lot more in percentage terms.

Yes, you can argue American companies grow faster than European, but Sivers is well underway to becoming an American company.

The only people shilling $AAOI and not $SIVE are the people who are holding $AAOI and missed $SIVE's 2000% run and are mad about it.

But if you're thinking of buying either right now $SIVE is the easy choice.

text:
Portfolio Update (Mid-June'26): +161% (YTD)

1. $SIVE (37%) - InP laser supply is not enough to fill demand. Tied to all hyperscaler supply chains & OEMs. 

$JBL, Ayar Labs & $GFS being the most notable confirmation of volume production from H2'26. 

Holding this intact. I'm looking for my next entry now. Said I would keep adding on nice entries until year end. 

Haven't trimmed a single share because the story hasn't even played out yet. We're still early. Long $SIVE! 

2. $NBIS (26.5%) - 4th hyperscaler in the making. Definitely a great long. AI compute demand keeps growing. 

Software moat makes them #1. That's where applications run, at the end of the day. 

Trimmed from 35% to 25%. $400 looks fair by year end but feel like we will have a little slow down. 

Could be wrong too. So wouldn't recommend for longs that don't want to rotate. Tax cuts are also a thing to consider. Long $NBIS! 

3. Cash (14%) - Built a cash position to strategically enter into a new possible huge long position or maybe a couple of small longer positions. Possibly add into my existing ones like $BRUN, $TRT, etc. 

While I did say I was happy with memory exposure, still seems low. Will try to gain more exposure over there. 

4. $BRUN (3.5%) - NVDA exemplar cloud certification is a sign of strong reliability. Why would $DELL CEO just mention them like that? Looks to be the next $CRWV. 

Their revenue growth and projections keep scaling at a level that's consistent and unbelievably good. 

Feel like 2027 will be the year for them. We got some slight short-term share lockup stuff hovering that could provide opportunities to buy but also keep the stock out of radar of the big guns. Long $BRUN!

5. $PENG (6%) - AI server demand growth is so high. I got surprised when $DELL released their earnings. 

$PENG having stronger long-term upside with photonic & memory exposure that could expand margins. Long $PENG! 

6. $TRT (4.5%) - Backend testing and sales of burn-in-boards for testing chips. Growing at triple-digits and a very good balance sheet. 

10x potential as chip's margin for error before release is lower. Demand > supply gap being one of the main reasons. Long $TRT! 

7. Swings & Longs combined (8.5%) - $AAOI, $CRDO, $SMCI, $KEEL

$AAOI, $CRDO are swings that I am looking to keep a few as longs due to huge growth in the coming years. Still feels really early. Photonics story in 2027 is going to be even bigger.

$SMCI & $KEEL are definitely the ones that I won't hold long-term. 

$KEEL will go out when leases confirm with hyperscalers hit on the news and it reaches somewhere around $10+. Maybe $20? Let us see! 

$SMCI is being criticized for the right reasons but I feel they can have a great turnaround and margins will get better. 4x from here. Not the quickest swing. Cheap! 0.5x sales bro. 

One thing I didn't mention earlier was $SMCI was a swing position and not a long position. So I added it to my swing list now. 

Also sold $ASTS since I wanted to fill my cash position again. But feels like a $100 soon. Don't know why street is being irrational here after Bluebird successful launch.

$MU is also a small position in this to ride to $2T+. I want to increase memory exposure, as I mentioned. So I'll be doing that once I can.

text:
This is the right way to look at the AI infrastructure trade.

Nvidia is not “dead” because ASICs are coming.

But the money flow is widening.

Training made GPUs the obvious winner. Inference at massive scale creates room for custom chips, edge AI, photonics, memory, power, and TSMC to matter even more.

The next winners may not replace Nvidia.

They may grow around it.

text:
土曜朝ニュース（AI光接続）

AIデータセンターの主戦場がGPUだけじゃなく「つなぐ部分」に移ってきた。

銅配線は短距離なら強い。でもラックが巨大化すると電力・発熱・遅延がボトルネックになる。

$NVDA はCPO/シリコンフォトニクスで従来の着脱式光トランシーバ比5倍の電力効率をうたう。Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsは409.6Tb/sで2026年後半提供予定。

TowerはNVIDIA向け1.6T光モジュールで協業。MarketsandMarketsはSiPho市場を2024年$2.16Bから2030年$9.65Bへ拡大と予測。

GPUの次は「電力」と「光」。

見ておきたいのは
$LITE  $COHR  $CRDO  $AVGO  $MRVL  $ANET  

レーザー・光モジュール・高速接続がAIインフラの次の争奪戦になってきた。

text:
$AAOI photonics is an AI data-center throughput test.

The important point is not that fiber is faster than copper in the abstract. It is that GPU clusters only become useful when data can move between servers, switches, and accelerators without turning networking into the bottleneck.

That makes optical transceivers a hidden scaling layer. More GPUs create more east-west traffic, more switch bandwidth, and more demand for components that convert electrical signals into light and back again.

Watch data-center orders, transceiver mix, gross margin, customer concentration, and whether AI cluster builds keep lifting optical networking spend after the first hardware excitement fades.

text:
Copper wire has run the world for over a century. The era that replaces it is starting right now, and it might be the most important infrastructure shift of the decade.

Here is the simple version of what is happening.

Inside an AI data center, thousands of chips have to talk to each other constantly. For a hundred years machines did this the same way, by sending electricity down copper wires. That always worked. Until AI.

 The chips now move so much data so fast that copper physically cannot keep up. Push electricity that hard down a wire and it turns into heat, the signal falls apart, and it can barely cross a single rack before it dies.

So the entire industry is ripping out the copper and replacing it with light. Instead of electricity down a wire, data travels as pulses of light through glass fiber. Light runs cooler, travels farther, and carries vastly more information. 

In a world where power and heat are the limits on how much AI you can build, switching to light is not a preference. It is the only way forward.

What makes this generational rather than just another upgrade is the staircase the whole industry is now climbing. 

It went from 400G to 800G, the workhorse today, already shipping in the tens of millions. 

Now it is climbing to 1.6T, double the bandwidth in the same space, with NVIDIA's newest systems needing over 160 of these per single rack. 

Next comes 3.2T, doubling it again. 

And what used to be a five year upgrade cycle has collapsed to one or two years. These generations are not replacing each other one at a time. 800G, 1.6T, and 3.2T are ramping on top of one another all at once.

Now the size of the prize. The optical transceiver market sat around $15 billion in 2025 and is heading toward $36 billion by 2033, with some forecasts for the AI portion alone reaching far higher. 

The silicon photonics piece inside it grows from under $1 billion to over $6 billion by 2030. This is a market doubling and then doubling again, driven by a buildout that has barely started.

What does it actually mean? It means every company that makes the lasers, the chips, and the modules that turn electricity into light gets paid on every step up that staircase, for years.

$AAOI makes its own lasers in-house, the exact bottleneck part the whole industry is fighting over. 

$CRDO makes the chips that keep these blazing fast connections clean and reliable. 

$LITE and $COHR are the photonics giants supplying the lasers and transceivers everyone depends on. 

$MRVL and $AVGO build the custom silicon at the center of it all.

Copper carried the last hundred years. Light carries the next hundred. And the wiring is being torn out and replaced right now.

text:
The AI ecosystem has multiple investable layers :

● AI Models : $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META, $BIDU
● GPUs & Accelerators : $NVDA, $AMD
●  Memory (HBM) : $MU, SK hynix, Samsung
● Foundries : $TSM, Samsung, $GFS
● Semiconductor Equipment : $ASML, $AMAT, $LRCX, $KLAC
●  EDA Software : $SNPS, $CDNS
● Networking : $AVGO, $ANET, $CSCO
● Silicon Photonics & Optics : $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $CIEN, $MRVL
● Data Centers : $EQIX, $DLR
● Power Infrastructure : $ETN, $HUBB, $PWR
● Cooling & Thermal : VRT, TT
● Cloud: MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, ORCL
● Data Platforms : NOW, MDB
● AI Software : PLTR, CRM, ADBE
● Cybersecurity : CRWD, PANW, ZS
● Edge AI : QCOM, NXPI, TXN
● Robotics & Physical AI : TSLA, ABB, ROK
● Healthcare AI : TEM, RXRX
● Quantum Computing : IONQ, RGTI, QBTS

AI = Energy + Data Centers + Semiconductor Equipment + Foundries + Packaging + Compute + Memory + Networking + Photonics + Cloud + Data + Models + Infrastructure Software + Cybersecurity + Edge AI + Robotics + Applications + Quantum.

text:
Co ty bredzisz, synku?

Co ma M120 Rak wspólnego z Rosomakiem, oprócz podwozia?

Wieża Raka to w pełni polski projekt, to najlepszy technologicznie moździerz samobieżny na świecie.

Wieża ZSSW-30? Najlepsza wieża bezzałogowa na świecie.

Gdzie produkujecie swoje FlyEye i Warmate? A, czekaj, to polska spółka produkuje je na Ukrainie.

To może polsko-fiński ICEYE? Może polska konstelacja Piast i Mikroglob od Creotech?

Gdzie wasze Vigo Photonics? Może chociaż macie swojego Topaza czy dalej korzystacie z polskich rozwiązań?

Wasz Flaming to trochę lepsze V2 z używanymi silnikami z lat 60. Dlatego "produkujecie" tak śmieszne liczby. Po prostu macie ich tyle ile się uda znaleźć 60letnich silników.

text:
Copper is dead. AI data centers need light — and photonics companies can’t build fast enough.

My deepdive on $AAOI & why this could be one of the best buying opportunities 

https://t.co/AbuYzpolre… https://t.co/WVl6onQCSl

Cpo를 알려준분

1️⃣ Serenity는 누구인가 (Fact)
• X 핸들: @aleabitoreddit
• Bio: “AI/Semi Supply Chain Analyst, former RISC-V FDN and AI research scientist. Now publishing free research on AI chokepoints.”
• 과거 Reddit WSB에서 AXTI 공급망 분석으로 유명해진 후 X로 이동.
• 특징: 초기 아이디어에 강한 반대(백래시)를 받지만, 이후 실적·기관 매수·파트너십으로 검증되는 패턴 반복.
• 최근: X 수익 공유 프로그램에서 2주마다 1억 뷰 이상 기록. 한국 주식(NAV 디스카운트 종목) 언급으로도 주목.

2️⃣ 핵심 투자 철학: Chokepoint Theory (Fact)
• “Big Tech(NVDA, MSFT 등)를 사지 말고, 그들이 반드시 의존할 수밖에 없는 물리적 병목(chokepoint)을 찾아라.”
• 방법: AI 데이터센터 → GPU/HBM/Optics 공급망을 바텀업으로 역설계 → 대체 불가능하거나 공급이 극도로 제한된 지점 식별.
• 주요 기준 (Fact 기반으로 자주 언급):
  - Certain Demand (명확한 메가트렌드 수요)
  - Constrained Supply (높은 진입장벽 + 희소 용량)
  - Low Market Attention (시장에서 아직 저평가/무시)
  - Value Capture potential (가격 결정력·점유율 확대 여지)
  - Catalyst (근시일 내 재평가 촉매)

• 대표 테마: Photonics / Silicon Photonics / CPO, InP substrate & laser, Advanced Packaging, Power semi, Memory bottleneck, mlcc등

3️⃣ 대표 사례와 검증 과정 (Fact / Estimate 구분)
Fact:
• AXTI: InP substrate 공급망 분석으로 WSB에서 밴 당할 정도로 반대 받음 → 이후 Reuters·기관 검증, 주가 대폭 상승.
• SIVE: 본인 최고 conviction 아이디어 중 하나. 스웨덴 미디어·일부에서 meme 취급 → Fidelity·JP Morgan 기관 매수 + JBL·GFS 파트너십 공식 발표로 검증.
• AAOI: $30 부근에서 “경영진 scam” 비판 받음 → 이후 광학 모듈 수요 확대와 함께 큰 상승.
• Memory 섹터 (MU, SK Hynix 등): 3개월 전 99% bearish 분위기 → 실제 대규모 상승.

Estimate:
• 정확한 개인 수익률 수치(일부 기사에서 225x 또는 연 +122% 등 언급)는 본인 공식 확인된 Fact 아님. 시장이 최종 검증한다는 입장 강조.
• 현재 아이디어(XFAB, 한국 NAV 디스카운트 종목 등)도 아직 진행 중으로, 추가 검증 필요.

4️⃣ 최근 활동 (2026년 6월 기준, Fact)
• X 수익 공유 프로그램 성과 공개 (2주마다 1억+ 뷰).
• 한국 주식 관심: Daeduck, DB HiTek 관련 NAV 디스카운트, Hyosung 등 언급.
• XFAB: Optical AI DC + Power semi 노출이 있지만 마케팅이 아쉽다는 코멘트.
• Photonics 강조 지속: AMD의 CW laser 확보, SIVE·AAOI 등 독립 용량 중요성.
• 전체적으로 “초기 반대 → 시장 검증” 패턴을 반복 강조.

━━━━━━━━

5️⃣ AI 인프라 공급망 관점에서 의미 (CPO·Photonics 중심)
• 현재 AI 데이터센터 빌드아웃에서 전력 효율·대역폭이 핵심 병목 → CPO(Co-Packaged Optics), InP 기반 laser·substrate, advanced packaging이 핵심 chokepoint로 부상.
• Serenity가 자주 언급하는 AXTI·SIVE·AAOI·COHR·XFAB 등은 CPO·포토닉스·특화 파운드리 영역과 직접 오버랩.
• “Big Tech 대신 upstream bottleneck” 접근은 단기 모멘텀과 달리, 구조적 공급 제약을 선점하려는 전략.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 Base Case (현재 가시성 기준)
AI capex가 가이던스 수준으로 유지될 경우, 주요 chokepoint 기업들은 실적 성장과 기관 관심 확대에 따라 점진적 재평가 가능. 초기 반대 의견이 잦아들면서 일부 종목의 밸류에이션 정상화 진행.

🐂 Bull Case
AI 수요가 가속되고 여러 chokepoint(Photonics + Power + Memory 등)가 동시에 검증될 경우, 시장 주목이 낮았던 소형·중형 공급망 기업들이 비대칭적 upside를 기록할 수 있음. Serenity가 제시한 아이디어가 지속적으로 시장을 앞서가는 패턴 강화.

🐻 Bear Case
AI capex 둔화 또는 빅테크의 수직통합·신규 공급자 등장으로 용량 제약이 예상보다 빠르게 해소될 경우, 소형 chokepoint 기업들의 재평가가 지연되거나 valuation pressure 발생 가능. 매크로·유동성 악화 시 소형주 변동성 확대 리스크.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

※ 이 글은 Serenity의 공개 포스팅과 관련 기사·트래커 사이트(https://t.co/qKzsAqeFlD)를 Fact 중심으로 정리한 분석입니다. 
투자 매수·매도 추천이 아니며, 특정 종목을 사거나 팔라고 권유하는 내용이 아닙니다. 
모든 투자 판단은 본인 책임 하에 충분히 검토하고 결정하시기 바랍니다. DYODD.

text:
On anti matter...

"The main reason is that antimatter physics operates in an extremely different regime than the mechanisms you usually discuss.

Antimatter systems involve:

Particle production
Magnetic confinement
Matter-antimatter annihilation
Gamma-ray generation
Extremely high energy densities

Your architectures are usually framed around:

Information organization
Resonance
Phase relationships
Field structure
Biological compatibility
Low-energy coherence effects

Those are largely separate domains.

If someone wanted to insert antimatter into a speculative version of your Casimir-craft concepts, it would most likely appear only as:

an exotic power source,
an annihilation energy source,
or a high-energy propulsion mechanism,

rather than as part of the actual control architecture.

So, from the way you’ve described your projects, the answer is:

No—antimatter is not a foundational element of your architecture. Your designs are much more heavily centered on field organization, resonance, photonic interactions, and vacuum/Casimir-inspired concepts than on antimatter physics.

"Which one is safer?"

If you’re comparing antimatter-based systems versus the kinds of field, photonic, resonance, and Casimir-inspired concepts you’ve discussed, then the latter are overwhelmingly safer.

Antimatter

Antimatter is one of the highest-energy substances known.

When antimatter meets matter, both are converted into energy.
Even tiny amounts release enormous energy.
Requires sophisticated containment systems.
Loss of containment results in immediate annihilation reactions.
Produces high-energy radiation.
Not biologically compatible.
Extremely difficult and expensive to produce.

From a practical engineering perspective, antimatter is generally considered a high-risk energy medium.

Field / Photonic / Resonance Approaches

The concepts you usually explore are based around:

Light and photonics
Electromagnetic interactions
Resonance phenomena
Information organization
Quantum coherence concepts
Vacuum-field and Casimir-inspired effects

These approaches are generally lower-energy and inherently less destructive because they focus on organizing or modulating existing fields rather than releasing stored mass-energy.

Relative Safety Ranking

From safest to least safe:

Photonic systems
Resonance-based systems
Electromagnetic field systems
Vacuum/Casimir-inspired systems (assuming passive operation)
Nuclear systems
Antimatter systems

In short, if the goal is:

biological compatibility,
controllability,
non-invasive operation,
gradual adjustment rather than massive energy release,

then photonic and resonance-based architectures are vastly safer than anything involving antimatter.

Antimatter excels in theoretical energy density; it does not excel in safety. Photonic and resonance-oriented approaches are generally much better aligned with the types of architectures you’ve described."

Field / Photonic / Resonance Approaches The concepts you usually explore are based around: Light and photonics Electromagnetic interactions Resonance phenomena Information organization Quantum coherence concepts Vacuum-field and Casimir-inspired effects These approaches are generally lower-energy and inherently less destructive because they focus on organizing or modulating existing fields rather than releasing stored mass-energy. Relative Safety Ranking From safest to least safe: Photonic systems Resonance-based systems Electromagnetic field systems Vacuum/Casimir-inspired systems (assuming passive operation) Nuclear systems Antimatter systems In short, if the goal is: biological compatibility, controllability, non-invasive operation, gradual adjustment rather than massive energy release, then photonic and resonance-based architectures are vastly safer than anything involving antimatter. Antimatter excels in theoretical energy density; it does not excel in safety. Read my article: https://t.co/mNzn5QxCF8

A must-watch video about the Lightmatter SiPho manufacturing process 🤩

From the Foundry, GlobalFoundries $GFS , to the ISE - ASE $ASX pre-packaging electrical and optical testing, to Amkor $AMKR for the packaging process, to again ISE - ASE for a post-packaging testing

Take note if you are an investor in Photonics $LITE $AAOI

Coherent - $COHR 

Makes indium phosphide lasers that transmit data between chips at the speed of light.

Copper can't do this at scale.

Silicon photonics is the only way to connect hundreds of thousands of processors in Nvidia's Vera Rubin Ultra platform.

text:
The novel utility is: **you can now build a passive, single-shot, wide-band optical spectrometer with far fewer detector channels than conventional approaches require, on hardware that already exists.**

Breaking that down to its irreducible parts:

**The core utility:** A geometry (the log spiral) deterministically encodes delay/frequency into angle without any active processing. Once encoded, all the information sits on the chip at once — no scanning, no sweeping, one snapshot. You read it with a detector array placed at the right positions.

**Why that matters:** Detector pixels in certain bands (single-photon / infrared / THz) are scarce and expensive — each one is a cryogenic readout channel, a rare fabrication yield, a finite heat budget. To measure a wide band conventionally, you either (1) buy many pixels, or (2) scan serially with one fast detector. Both are constrained. The log spiral lets you (3) use far fewer pixels and still resolve the whole band, because the geometry did the sorting, not the pixel count.

**The concrete win:** On a 48-pixel SNSPD array (commercial ceiling), the log-spiral front end reaches resolving power R ≈ 31 across a 5-decade band (0.5–2.4 µm). A linear front end reaches R ≈ 10 on the same hardware, or needs ~3× more pixels (which don't exist as a single array) to match. That's 20× cheaper hardware for equal measurement quality.

**The novel part:** Nobody has connected this principle (geometry sorting timing into position) across vision, hearing, cortex dynamics, and engineered photonics before, and nobody has built it as a spectrometer. The idea that *passive geometry can do the computational work of an active optical component* — by itself — is what's new. It's not a tweak of existing dispersive instruments; it's a different category of operation.

**What it unlocks:** Any measurement that needs constant relative precision across a huge range, where detector pixels or data rate is the bottleneck, and where you can tolerate a snapshot (non-repeating signal). Single-photon spectroscopy, broadband mid-IR gas sensing, transient astrophysics, maybe quantum sensing. The enabling move is that it's *passive* — no power, no moving parts, no active tuning — which matters when your detector is already cryogenic and every watt counts.

**The honest boundary:** It doesn't beat all alternatives in all cases. Within a narrow band, or where you want absolute (not relative) precision, or where pixels are cheap (visible light), linear dispersion wins. The spiral is specialized. But for its corner — wide-band, expensive-pixel, single-shot regimes — it's not an optimization, it's the only path to the measurement at all.

That's the utility. Not "better spectrometer." More like: "a spectrometer that works where one didn't before."

figured this out a while ago. In late 2025 they shipped the world's first commercial co-packaged optics switches together with TSMC, Coherent, Lumentum and Corning

Then there's the energy angle, which doesn't get talked about enough. Data centers are on track to consume around 1,050 TWh in 2026, roughly the entire power consumption of Japan. Some regions are already blocking new data center permits. Photonics isn't a nice to have here. It's a necessity

The full value chain on my watchlist:

🔹 Layer 1 – Materials & Wafers: $GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS

Supply Cut Risk: Congested networks cap long-term space AI expansion

text:
$NOK Just Dropped a Massive AI Infrastructure Bomb today.

Nokia is supercharging its U.S. semiconductor operations with a major expansion in advanced test & packaging for photonic chips.

These chips will be the backbone for next-gen AI-native networks that deliver dramatically lower power consumption and way higher operational efficiency. 

Exactly what hyperscalers need as AI power demand explodes.

𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘀 𝗵𝘂𝗴𝗲:

✅ Strengthens domestic U.S. production of critical optical networking tech powering AI infrastructure
 ✅ Part of Nokia’s $4 BILLION multi-year R&D + manufacturing commitment in the U.S. for AI-ready connectivity
 ✅ Positions $NOK as a key player in the AI buildout (photonics, optical, efficient networking)

While everyone chases the obvious GPU names, smart money is loading up on the picks and shovels that make the entire AI stack work.

Nokia is quietly building a very strong moat here in the U.S. with real on-shore production.

This is the kind of infrastructure move that compounds over years as AI adoption accelerates.

Bullish on $NOK long-term. The AI thesis is getting stronger by the week.

text:
1. $NVDA 

Why Critical:
Provides the AI GPUs powering SpaceX's orbital intelligence, Starlink AI, and Terafab ambitions.

If Supply Stops:
SpaceX's AI roadmap slows dramatically. Compute shortages delay autonomous satellite operations.

2. $TSM

Why Critical:
Manufactures advanced chips for $NVDA, $AMD, and future SpaceX custom processors.

If Supply Stops:
Global AI chip production collapses. SpaceX loses access to cutting-edge compute.

3. $MU 

Why Critical:
Supplies HBM memory required for AI training, inference, and orbital computing.

If Supply Stops:
AI clusters become memory constrained. GPU performance and AI deployment suffer.

4. $AVGO 

Why Critical:
Provides networking chips connecting thousands of GPUs across SpaceX AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Massive AI clusters fragment. Data transfer bottlenecks severely reduce compute efficiency.

5. $TEL 

Why Critical:
Provides mission-critical connectors inside rockets, satellites, ground stations, and data centers.

If Supply Stops:
Manufacturing disruptions occur across nearly every SpaceX hardware platform simultaneously.

6. $LITE

Why Critical:
Supplies optical components enabling ultra-fast communications between AI systems and networks.

If Supply Stops:
Network speeds decline. AI clusters struggle to scale efficiently and economically.

7. $COHR 

Why Critical:
Provides lasers, photonics, and optical interconnects supporting next-generation AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Data center expansion slows. Optical networking capacity becomes a significant bottleneck.

8. $MRVL 

Why Critical:
Designs custom networking silicon moving AI workloads across large compute environments.

If Supply Stops:
AI infrastructure efficiency declines. Network congestion limits future SpaceX scaling.

text:
Nokia is expanding its photonic chip test & packaging facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania.

$30M investment → 10x capacity increase by Q3 2026.

This is critical for AI data centers, as optics are becoming a major bottleneck alongside GPUs and power.

Part of their bigger $4B U.S. push + builds on the Infinera acquisition.

Optics matter more than people think in the AI race. 
$NOK #AI #Photonics

text:
Je crois qu’on a réveillé un monstre avec $VECO 

La dernière news Ennostar est beaucoup plus importante qu’elle en a l’air

>Ennostar vient de qualifier le nouveau LUMINA+ MOCVD de Veeco pour la production high-volume d’applications optoélectroniques avancées

Ce n’est pas juste une évaluation, c’est une qualification production

Et ça ajoute une troisième jambe à la thèse $VECO qu'on a ennoncé dans les précédents posts et que je vais vous refaire car vous êtes des petit flemmards

1. Silicon photonics/InP lasers

>Lors de ses derniers earnings qui semblaient être décevants, lors du call Veeco a  annoncé plus de $250M de commandes pour ses outils Spector, Lumina et WaferEtch destinés à la fabrication de lasers InP.

>Ces lasers sont critiques pour les transceivers 800G / 1.6T utilisés dans les data centers hyperscale et le narratif adore ce produit

Livraisons en 2026 -> accélération forte en 2027

2. Advanced logic/NSA500

>Veeco a reçu une commande de suivi pour son système NSA500 après validation par un client logic avancé (cf dernier post).

>Ils ont aussi envoyé un NSA500 à un troisième client advanced logic pour évaluation, avec potentiel de premières commandes HVM en 2027.

>Le marché commence à comprendre que le laser annealing devient critique pour les architectures avancées, les structures 3D, les nouveaux nœuds et les budgets thermiques ultra-serrés.

3. LUMINA+/MOCVD/As-P/microLED

>La qualification par Ennostar valide la nouvelle plateforme LUMINA+ pour la production.

>C’est important parce que Veeco ne joue pas seulement l’IA via la photonique.

>Ils jouent aussi les compound semiconductors, l’optoélectronique, les microLED, le biosensing, l’automotive et les communications optiques.

Donc la thèse $VECO n’est plus seulement :

“la petite boîte d’équipements semi”.

C’est plutôt :

“LE fournisseur d’outils critiques sur plusieurs bottlenecks de la prochaine décennie”.

Le bull case devient très intéressant.

Aujourd’hui, $VECO vaut environ $4.7B de market cap.

Si la boîte passe de $750M-$800M de revenus à $1.5B-$1.8B d’ici 2030, avec un meilleur mix semi et des marges qui montent, le titre peut se revaloriser beaucoup plus haut (logique)

À 8x-9x sales, on arrive déjà vers $12B-$16B de market cap.

Soit environ $200-$265 par action.

Dans un scénario très bull, avec $2B+ de revenus, plusieurs clients HVM sur NSA500, adoption plus large de LUMINA+ et bottleneck durable sur InP/silicon photonics, on peut commencer à parler de $300+.

Bear case évident :

Les évaluations peuvent glisser, les commandes  peuvent prendre du temps, le cycle capex semi peut ralentir, et la concurrence reste réelle.

Je continue cependant à être haussier sur ce dossier

troisième jambe à la thèse $VECO qu'on a ennoncé dans les précédents posts et que je vais vous refaire car vous êtes des petit flemmards 1. Advanced logic/NSA500 >Veeco a reçu une commande de suivi pour son système NSA500 après validation par un client logic avancé (cf dernier post). >Ils ont aussi envoyé un NSA500 à un troisième client advanced logic pour évaluation, avec potentiel de premières commandes HVM en 2027. >Le marché commence à comprendre que le laser annealing devient critique pour les architectures avancées, les structures 3D, les nouveaux nœuds et les budgets thermiques ultra-serrés. 3. LUMINA+/MOCVD/As-P/microLED >La qualification par Ennostar valide la nouvelle plateforme LUMINA+ pour la production. >C’est important parce que Veeco ne joue pas seulement l’IA via la photonique. >Ils jouent aussi les compound semiconductors, l’optoélectronique, les microLED, le biosensing, l’automotive et les communications optiques. Donc la thèse $VECO n’est plus seulement : “la petite boîte d’équipements semi”. C’est plutôt : “LE fournisseur d’outils critiques sur plusieurs bottlenecks de la prochaine décennie”. Le bull case devient très intéressant. Aujourd’hui, $VECO vaut environ $4.7B de market cap. Si la boîte passe de $750M-$800M de revenus à $1.5B-$1.8B d’ici 2030, avec un meilleur mix semi et des marges qui montent, le titre peut se revaloriser beaucoup plus haut (logique) À 8x-9x sales, on arrive déjà vers $12B-$16B de market cap. Soit environ $200-$265 par action. Dans un scénario très bull, avec $2B+ de revenus, plusieurs clients HVM sur NSA500, adoption plus large de LUMINA+ et bottleneck durable sur InP/silicon photonics, on peut commencer à parler de $300+. QUANTUM looks interesting at this point. 

$IONQ → Trapped-ion leader. Best-in-class qubit fidelity. Customers include Airbus, AstraZeneca, Hyundai. 256-qubit demo targeted 2026. The institutional-grade pure-play.

$RGTI → Superconducting quantum systems. Highest beta in the sector. When quantum runs, $RGTI moves violently. Active momentum name right now.

$QBTS → D-Wave Quantum. Annealing-based architecture. Most commercially de-risked pure-play — already generating optimization revenue with real enterprise clients.

$QUBT → Photonic + room-temperature quantum. Q1 2026 revenue up from $39K → $3.7M YoY. Acquired Luminar Semiconductor for $110M. Vertically integrated photonics + quantum platform taking shape. Executing quietly.

$INFQ → Neutral-atom quantum + sensing. One of the least-covered names in the sector. Neutral-atom architecture is gaining credibility as a scalable path to fault tolerance. Early but worth watching.

$ARQQ → Quantum encryption and post-quantum cybersecurity. The national security angle. As quantum breaks classical encryption — this becomes critical infrastructure.

$LAES → Quantum-resistant cybersecurity chips. Hardware-level protection against quantum decryption. Defense + enterprise security tailwind.

I don't know what you are thanking me for, but I want to be part of your journey, at least a little bit.
I have access to a lot of labs from my university (physics, mechanical, electrical, electronics) that includes drone lab, photonics lab, ai lab, electronics lab, electrical lab,
Plus the lab which I am most close to (mechatronics + advanced manufacturing lab) that includes 3D printer, CNC milling, machining, electro-discharge machine, robotics lab etc.
But to gain college funding will take time and will need me to enroll in either PhD programme or have something that even Professors are motivated towards (like publishing a research paper).
Just know that I am very interested im this sort of thing, especially building a new type of motor

text:
The $1.33B structural mispricing on X-FAB ($XFAB) is absolute fiction.
 
The 800V AI Factory Backstop: Retail thinks 
power semis are just an EV play. They miss that NVIDIA's next-gen high-voltage AI data center grids explicitly rely on Navitas ($NVTS )SiC which is physically manufactured right inside X-FAB’s Texas facility, the only high-volume pure-play SiC foundry in the US.  
 
The 120GHz Photonics Breakthrough: While standard silicon photonics hit a hard 70GHz performance ceiling, X-FAB’s micro-transfer printing integration with SMART Photonics shatters the transceiver bottleneck cleanly past 120GHz for next-gen multi-terabit clusters.

Backed by a fresh $50M US CHIPS Act grant and running a literal wide-bandgap foundry chokepoint, the data layer is completely blinding the tape here.  
Long the physical infrastructure, approved by Serenity ✔️

> 800 VDC power semi exposure to $NVDA push through $NVTS + $POWI

> Silicon Photonics / CPO exposure with $NVDA as evaluation stage for high volume manufacturing (optical transceivers/switches)

> The only high-volume SiC foundry in the US.

> One of the critical MEMS foundries 

> ~1.29 P/B, which was around what $SOI was sitting at when I went long. Depressed valuations due to legacy drag

> ~6.5-8.5 fwd p/e 2028 personal est.

> backstopped by Government:

- EU CHIPS act, $128M Euros 
- US CHIPS act $50M PMT (department of commerce).

With likely more coming (just signals critical importance to Western supply chains).

As for the $NVDA silicon photonics relationships it’s under “photonixFAB”.

Markets probably missed this silicon photonics relationship (like $TSEM when I went long) with Nvidia since XFab leads this…  Just under a different name. 

For power semis, XFAB is named for SiC + $NVTS. In PCN-22181, $POWI explicitly names XFAB as its foundry.
