Quantum Microwave (USA): A massive distributor and manufacturer that packs and pairs LNF amplifiers with cryogenic attenuators and components specifically configured for quantum rigs AND MANY MORE. $AMPG is a cool company and I even thought about investing in them but this take is absolutely ridiculous, obvious exaggerated way to pump a stock or make it seem bigger than it is. text:
A lot of the AI infrastructure thesis from the past few weeks are starting to play out.

Neocloud / Sovereign AI:
$CRWV 100 → 121
$NBIS 228 → 297

Memory:
Still holding dram long from ~68, added more $SKHYNIX
$MU earnings next week could be another important read-through for HBM demand. (Jun 24)

Photonics:

China just approved the first major 2026 InP substrate export batch, providing some near-term relief to one of the optical bottlenecks discussed in my photonics thread.

I’m still watching this closely. Demand for 800G/1.6T AI interconnects continues to explode, so a single export batch doesn’t solve the structural shortage, it simply unlocks some capacity. The real question is whether supply can keep up with AI cluster buildouts over the next 12–24 months.

Meanwhile:
• HBM4E sampling next month
• SK Hynix US ADR listing
• SK Hynix earnings (Jul 23)
• Ongoing sovereign AI buildouts globally

The AI trade keeps moving up the stack.

First GPUs.
Then neoclouds.
Then photonics.
Now memory.

The biggest winners continue to come from identifying the bottlenecks before the market fully prices them in.
embedded_text:

$NBIS was one of the clearest beneficiaries of the Anthropic/Mythos developments and the broader sovereign AI buildout.

$CRWV continues to benefit from the same

text:
🇺🇸 | نتائج توصيات السوق الأمريكي اليوم

يوم جديد يؤكد أن قوة التداول ليست في كثرة الصفقات، بل في دقة الاختيار وإدارة الخروج.

تم اليوم إغلاق صفقتين على أرباح قوية:

✅سهم Laser Photonics LASE
من 2.38 إلى 2.65
ربح محقق: +11.3%

✅سهم Roblox RBLX
من 43 إلى 49.13
ربح محقق: +14.3%

وفي المقابل، تم إغلاق صفقة ResMed RMD عند وقف الخسارة بخسارة محدودة تقارب -2%، وهذا جزء طبيعي من أي خطة تداول منضبطة.

📍الخلاصة:
نترك الأرباح تكبر عندما تسير الصفقة في صالحنا، ونغلق الخسارة مبكرًا عندما لا تؤكد الحركة توقعاتنا.

💥هكذا تُبنى النتائج:
ربح قوي عند تحقق الأهداف، وخسارة محدودة عند فشل السيناريو.

 📍في Stock Basket، نعتمد على الدمج بين إدارة الصفقة باحتراف، والبوت الذكي الذي يساعدنا في فرز وتصنيف الفرص داخل السوق قبل التنفيذ.

👈 تفضل واحجز مكانك لمتابعة صفقات واضحة بمستويات دخول، أهداف، حماية ربح، وإدارة دقيقة أثناء الجلسة، 
-الانضمام للمجموعة الذهبية هو الخطوة الأقرب لك.

📩 للاشتراك والتفاصيل، راسلنا الآن.

⚠️ إخلاء مسؤولية:
جميع المعلومات لأغراض إعلامية فقط وليست توصية مباشرة. يُرجى الرجوع إلى مستشار مالي مرخّص قبل أي قرار.

text:
Not claiming $SIVE builds the plasmonic modulator itself.

The point is that plasmonics does not remove the need for high-performance laser sources it makes the optical input layer even more critical.

That is exactly where Sivers is positioned: high-power DFB laser arrays, external light sources and silicon-photonics integration for AI datacenters / CPO.

And this is now supported by the GlobalFoundries $GFS collaboration, where Sivers’ laser arrays are being integrated into reference designs on GF’s silicon photonics platform, including SCALE for next-gen optical sub-assemblies.

So yes from a system architecture perspective, this is highly relevant to $SIVE

text:
It used to take a carefully controlled lab setup to shape light with precision. Now, that same level of control is moving into real-world systems. 

Spatial light modulators, or SLMs, are evolving to be faster, more stable, and ready for deployment—helping engineers fine-tune light for everything from advanced imaging to quantum applications. 

Hamamatsu’s Brandon Gorakhnauth shares his insights on how what was once experimental is becoming essential and where photonics is headed next. 🔗 https://t.co/jdRyuOvn4e 

#Photonics #Optics #SLM #Engineering #Innovation

text:
Contemporary silicon photonics modulators are constrained by the diffraction limit of light, restricting their ability to scale efficiently to the bandwidths AI infrastructure will require. Plasmonics offers a path forward.

By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems.

Marvell Senior Director Claudia Hoessbacher and Director Wolfgang Heni of Optical Engineering detail the technology, its applications across scale-up and scale-across networks, and where development stands today.

Read more: https://t.co/3z9yPJxzac

VIGO PHOTONICS SPÓŁKA AKCYJNA (WIG80-#VGO)----19:57----

VIGO Photonics planuje emisję do 131.219 akcji serii G i możliwą sprzedaż do 124.800 akcji przez głównego akcjonariusza

Potencjalny wpływ: Istotny

This is one of those announcements that’s easy to overlook but could become much bigger if it plays out.

NUBURU isn’t just talking about lasers anymore. It’s positioning its blue-laser technology for underwater power infrastructure through the DEEP LIGHT project, which is backed by the Italian Navy. That’s an interesting combination of photonics, defense, and critical infrastructure.

The key thing to remember is that this is still subject to final agreements and technical validation, so it’s not guaranteed revenue yet. But if the project moves into commercialization, it could open the door to a much larger market than many investors are currently pricing in.

Definitely one to keep on the watchlist. $BURU

General Serino’s experience and insight bring significant value as we continue advancing our defense and security strategy, including the planned acquisition of Tekne and the expansion of our broader ecosystem across defense mobility, electronic warfare, critical infrastructure protection, operational resilience, and next-generation directed-energy technologies.

The participation of leaders with proven military and defense expertise reinforces our belief that successful execution requires not only innovative technologies, but also strategic guidance from individuals who understand the evolving needs of modern defense organizations.

text:
That Jabil print is a massive wake-up call for anyone who thinks AI infrastructure spending is cooling off.

While the broader market has been sweating over whether hyperscale capex is going to slow down, Jabil’s Q3 numbers and raised guidance prove that the physical buildout is actually accelerating. When a manufacturing giant that handles the servers, networking, and data center assembly for the biggest players in the world lifts its outlook, it’s a direct proxy for what's coming downstream.
Here is the reality for the optical and photonics space:

The Capex Slowdown Is a Myth: Jabil didn't just beat earnings; they raised their Q4 revenue guidance to a massive $9.2B–$10.0B. CEO Mike Dastoor explicitly stated that AI infrastructure demand is "extremely strong" and pushed their full-year AI revenue outlook meaningfully higher. The physical hardware layer is getting plugged in as fast as they can build it.

The Institutional Trigger for $SIVE: Pushing past the 100 SEK ($10) mark is a psychological and structural milestone. For a lot of institutional funds, crossing that threshold clears the internal liquidity mandates that previously kept them on the sidelines. It opens up a completely new class of buyer for the stock.

Direct Read-Through for $AAOI : Jabil's raised outlook signals a massive volume ramp in networking components. You cannot scale these next-gen AI clusters without an enormous amount of optical transceivers and advanced routing hardware, which directly backstops the demand runway for independent component suppliers.

The market generalists are still debating macro sentiment, but the actual corporate data shows the physical backbone of AI is hitting maximum utilization.
Still hyper-bullish the optical super-cycle. 

Long $SIVE and $AAOI.

text:
Tower, Marvell pass five million coherent photonic ICs

Tower Semiconductor (NASDAQ/TASE: TSEM) and Marvell Technology have shipped more than five million coherent photonic integrated circuits built on Tower's silicon photonics platform, the Israeli foundry said Thursday from Migdal Haemek. The chips are used in coherent optical modules for data center interconnects, where they support higher bandwidth and lower power per bit than legacy approaches. Tower manufactures the photonic ICs at its fabs and supplies them to Marvell, which integrates them into its coherent digital signal processor-based optical products. Neither company disclosed the value of the shipments or named end customers. (Tower Semiconductor)

Vik:
This announcement is only one thing: a flex to show who is building more SiPho chips right now. Googled "GF shipments SiPho" and came up with 3 substacks (one was mine). Flex is working.

@austinsemis @vikramskr

text:
7/
Net view:

Power and memory are still binding constraints today.

Interconnects and silicon photonics are the next layer the market is beginning to price in.

The companies best positioned sit at the intersection of InP lasers ( $LITE , $COHR ), advanced packaging ( $TSM ), and AI networking demand ( $NVDA , $MRVL , $AVGO ).

2026 is shaping up as the first year of meaningful commercial CPO ramps. The supply chain is tight, but the direction is clear: light is replacing copper for the highest-bandwidth links in AI infrastructure.

Not financial advice. This is for informational purposes only.

text:
5/
$TSM COUPE is emerging as the central integration platform.
•  65nm silicon photonics base already in volume production
•  COUPE (Compact Universal Photonic Engine) uses advanced stacking to combine electrical and photonic dies
•  Targeted for meaningful CPO volume in 2026
•  $NVDA is already shipping early TSMC-backed CPO switches

As CPO moves from pluggables into co-packaged and deeper integration, TSMC’s packaging capacity becomes a key gating factor.

text:
3/
The silicon photonics supply chain (AI data center focus):

Upstream
•  SOI wafers (base substrate)
•  Indium Phosphide (InP) — the critical material for high-performance lasers. No scalable substitute exists today.

Lasers & Active Components
•  InP-based EMLs and continuous-wave lasers
•  Key names: $LITE (Lumentum) and $COHR (Coherent)

Design & Fabrication
•  Photonic Integrated Circuits (PICs)
•  $TSM (TSMC) COUPE platform is currently leading for AI-relevant integration

Advanced Packaging & CPO
•  This is becoming the new bottleneck layer
•  TSMC CoWoS + COUPE stacking for co-packaged optics

Downstream
•  High-speed transceivers (800G/1.6T+)
•  Optical switches and fabrics (NVIDIA, Broadcom, Marvell) $NVDA $MRVL $AVGO

text:
2/
Why this layer matters now:

As AI clusters grow to tens of thousands of GPUs, the amount of data that needs to move between chips explodes. When accelerators sit idle waiting for data, the economics break.

Silicon photonics moves data with light instead of electricity. It delivers higher bandwidth density, lower power per bit, and longer reach than copper.
The shift from traditional pluggable optics to CPO is accelerating in 2026.

Power and Memory (HBM).

Copper is hitting its physical limits on bandwidth, reach, and power. The industry’s answer is silicon photonics + Co-Packaged Optics (CPO). $LITE $AAOI $NVDA

text:
$CRDO vs $AVGO – Analysis Summary
Based on the referenced Zacks Article 

The article favors CRDO over AVGO at present due to stronger growth, earnings
estimate revisions, and AI networking exposure.

Bullish Factors for CRDO
• Fiscal 2026 revenue grew more than 200% year-over-year.
• Management expects strong growth into fiscal 2027.
• Strong demand for Active Electrical Cables (AECs) from hyperscalers and AI infrastructure providers.
• Dust Photonics acquisition expands optical connectivity capabilities.
• High gross margins and operating leverage support profitability.

Why AVGO Remains Attractive
• More diversified business model.
• Significant cash flow generation.
• Strong AI customer relationships.
• Generally viewed as the lower-risk alternative.

Comparison
Growth Rate: CRDO Advantage
AI Networking Exposure: CRDO Advantage
Stability & Diversification: AVGO Advantage
Cash Flow Strength: AVGO Advantage
Upside Potential: CRDO Advantage
Downside Protection: AVGO Advantage

Perspective
The article reinforces the thesis that CRDO is evolving into a major AI networking platform.

Continued success in AECs, optical connectivity, and integration of Dust Photonics could support
ongoing growth. For covered-call investors, the primary risk is that the stock continues to outperform and requires rolling calls higher to avoid assignment.

Strategy
Premium members only…(omitted any further strategy)

The key question is whether CRDO's earnings growth will justify substantially.

text:
I was watching a Bloomberg video on YouTube about space-based data centers, and a lightbulb suddenly went off in my head: Could Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE lasers actually benefit from this trend?

Here is my attempt to map out the thesis and connect the dots.

1/ How do you even build a data center in space?
It sounds like sci-fi, but it’s becoming the next major frontier as Elon Musk has openly discussed it with Starlink, and a startup called StarCloud has already launched the first GPU-equipped satellite into orbit. They’ve even successfully run an Nvidia H100 in space to train an LLM for the very first time.

The core idea is to bypass Earth's strained power grids entirely. By putting data centers directly into orbit, you get uninterrupted, 24/7 solar energy without weather or night-cycle disruptions. Instead of massive air conditioning units, these satellites dump heat using specialized radiators into the vacuum of space.

However, a single satellite is essentially just one GPU rack. To function like a true, high-performance AI data center, you can't have isolated chips floating in silos. You need entire swarms of these satellites linked together in real time, functioning as one giant, distributed supercomputer cluster.

2/ Why lasers are the only viable solution
To make these orbital clusters work, you need massive bandwidth on a strict power budget. Lasers are the only technology that checks both boxes.

Think of data transfer like a water hose: the width of the hose determines how much water (data) you can move, and that width is dictated by the frequency of the wave you use.

Radio Waves (The Old Way): Traditional satellites use radio frequencies. It’s a low-frequency, wide beam that is easy to aim, but it's the equivalent of a garden hose. It's perfectly fine for simple commands or beam-down TV signals, but it completely chokes on the terabits-per-second traffic that a GPU cluster generates internally. Plus, to get anywhere near the required bandwidth with radio, you’d need massive, heavy antennas—and every extra kilogram launched into space costs a fortune.

Lasers / Optical (The New Way): Light operates at a vastly higher frequency, meaning the "hose" is massive. You can cram an incredible amount of data through a laser beam. It’s the exact same reason our home internet upgraded from copper wires to fiber optics.

In space, power is everything because you are limited to what your solar panels and batteries can generate. Lasers are incredibly efficient, delivering vastly more data per watt than radio. While the engineering challenge is steep, you have to precisely aim a laser pointer across thousands of km of vacuum, the payoff is undeniable. Radio will always have a place for low-stakes backup communications because it's reliable, but for the heavy-duty data shifting an AI cluster demands, lasers are the only option that fits both the bandwidth and the weight budget.

3/ Where does Sivers $SIVE fit into this?
This is where the investment thesis gets interesting. Sivers explicitly lists SATCOM (Satellite Communications) as one of its core target markets, right alongside terrestrial AI data centers. It’s the exact same underlying laser and photonics platform, just packaged for orbital links instead of ground-based server racks.

To be clear: I haven’t found any official announcements, contracts, or partnerships linking Sivers to Starcloud, Crusoe, or any specific space-data-center project yet.

But here is the thesis: If these satellite swarms scale up the way Musk, Bezos, and major tech investors are betting they will, someone has to supply the advanced photonics hardware. Sivers is one of only a handful of companies with proven, high-power DFB (Distributed Feedback) laser technology sitting right at the intersection of AI optics and satellite communication.

There's no signed contract on paper today, but given how fast this market is moving, it’s absolutely a name to keep on your radar.
$SIVE

text:
Every stock looks obvious in hindsight.

Nobody gets rewarded for buying after the thesis is universally accepted.

The biggest winners are often the stocks that look boring, misunderstood, or outright wrong at the start.

- People laughed at semicap before AI.
- People ignored photonics before bandwidth became the bottleneck.
- People dismissed power semis before power efficiency became the bottleneck.

Today, $XFAB gets labeled as "just another automotive foundry."

Market is once again underestimating where the next bottleneck is forming: power, SiC, sensors, and specialty manufacturing.

We'll find out soon enough.

Markets don't care about consensus.

text:
🚨🚨🚨📢

$SPX $SPY $QQQ $IWM $VIX

While everyone is focused on Trump playing geopolitical ping pong with Iran, I'm watching AI and semiconductors inflate what may become the mother of all bubbles.

The real story isn't today's headlines.

It's the $700B to $750B annual AI infrastructure arms race quietly unfolding beneath the surface. 👇

1/
The hyperscalers, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta, are spending like drunken sailors with unlimited credit cards.

Annual AI CapEx is projected to reach $700B to $750B.

Global AI related debt issuance could exceed $500B.

They're borrowing against the future and praying the future arrives on schedule.

2/
The biggest risk?

Stranded capital.

Today they're borrowing billions to fill data centers with power hungry Nvidia GPUs.

The problem is those assets are depreciated over 5 to 6 years.

Technology may make them obsolete in 2 to 3.

That's not innovation.

That's a future write off waiting for an earnings call.

3/
Imagine owing bondholders hundreds of billions while sitting on warehouses full of yesterday's hardware.

Silicon photonics.

Neuromorphic computing.

Custom ASICs.

If these technologies arrive faster than expected, today's AI infrastructure could become the corporate equivalent of buying a fleet of steam engines right before electricity.

4/
The spending frenzy also reveals another problem.

Free cash flow.

CapEx is consuming an extraordinary share of revenue:

• Alphabet about 46%
• Microsoft about 47%
• Meta about 54%

The more they spend, the more cash flow gets squeezed.

They're running faster just to stay in place.

5/
This is why everyone wants their own chips.

Google TPUs.

Amazon Trainium.

Meta custom silicon.

The goal isn't just performance.

The goal is survival.

They need lower operating costs to justify the debt they're piling onto their balance sheets today.

6/
Then comes the next twist.

If AI chips become dramatically more efficient, power stops being the bottleneck.

Location becomes the advantage.

Data centers may no longer need to sit next to massive power sources.

They can move closer to population centers, reducing latency and potentially turning today's expensive power agreements into tomorrow's capital allocation mistakes.

7/
And then there's the risk nobody wants to discuss.

The Inference Slump.

The entire AI spending boom assumes enterprise demand explodes.

But many AI pilot programs still struggle to generate meaningful returns.

If AI inference becomes extremely cheap, compute becomes a commodity.

And commodities don't generate bubble valuations.

8/
That's the paradox.

The hyperscalers need hyper efficient chips to save themselves.

But those same chips could eventually destroy the economics supporting today's AI infrastructure boom.

The cure may become the disease.

9/
Ultimately, Big Tech is engaged in a historic game of financial chicken.

They're borrowing hundreds of billions today.

Betting that tomorrow's technology arrives before today's debt becomes a problem.

Wall Street calls it innovation.

History often calls it a bubble.

Choose carefully which chapter we're currently writing.

text:
Bir başka diğer bahsetmek istediğim şirket ise ;

$COHR 

Anlık Fiyat :389 #dolar

COHR Tam Olarak Ne İş Yapıyor?

Coherent Corp.

Coherent, dünyanın en büyük fotonik (ışık teknolojileri) şirketlerinden biridir.

Basit anlatırsak:

NVDA hesap yapıyor.✅

 MU veriyi depoluyor.✅

 COHR ise bu verinin veri merkezinde ışıkla taşınmasını sağlıyor. ✅

Yani yapay zekanın sinir sistemi diyebileceğimiz katmanda yer alıyor. (İşte en can alıcı nokta bu )

Şirketin Ana İş Kolları : 

1. Optik Transceiver'lar

Bunlar veri merkezlerinde kullanılan özel modüller.

Görevleri:

GPU → GPU
Sunucu → Sunucu
Rack → Rack

arasındaki veriyi ışıkla taşımak.

AI modelleri büyüdükçe bu ürünlerin önemi katlanıyor. ⚡️⚡️

2. Silicon Photonics

Bence şirketin en kritik alanı.

Elektrik sinyallerini ışığa dönüştürüyor.

Avantajları:

Daha az enerji tüketimi✅

Daha yüksek hız✅

Daha düşük gecikme✅

Daha az ısı✅

NVIDIA'nın gelecek nesil veri merkezi mimarisinde tam olarak ihtiyaç duyulan teknoloji bu.

3. Lazerler

COHR dünyanın en büyük endüstriyel ve telekom lazer üreticilerinden biri.

Ürettikleri:

InP (Indium Phosphide) lazerler
CW lazerler
VCSEL lazerler
Telekom lazerleri

Bu lazerler hem kendi ürünlerinde hem de sektörün diğer oyuncularında kullanılıyor.

4. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO)

Şu anda Wall Street'in en heyecanlandığı konu.

Bugün:

GPU ↔ Optik modül ayrı.

Yarın:

GPU ↔ Optik modül aynı pakette olacak.

Bu teknolojiye CPO deniyor.

NVIDIA'nın gelecekteki veri merkezi planlarının merkezinde bu teknoloji var. 

COHR da burada ana tedarikçilerden biri olmaya çalışıyor.

Kimlerle Çalışıyor?

Kesin müşteri listeleri her zaman açıklanmıyor ancak sektör analizlerinde ve şirket sunumlarında şu isimler sürekli karşımıza çıkıyor:

NVIDIA
Microsoft Azure
Amazon Web Services
Meta
Google Cloud

Çünkü bunların hepsi devasa AI veri merkezleri kuruyor ve optik ağ altyapısına ihtiyaç duyuyorlar.

NVIDIA ile İlişkisi

Burası çok önemli.

2026'da $NVDA :

Coherent'e yaklaşık 2 milyar dolar yatırım yaptı.

Uzun vadeli satın alma anlaşmaları yaptı.

Lazer ve optik bileşen kapasitesini garanti altına aldı.

Bu haber çıktığında COHR hissesi sert yükselmişti.

Çünkü bu durum:

"NVIDIA gelecekte fotoniğin kritik olacağını düşünüyor."

mesajını verdi.

Neden Yapay Zeka İçin Kritik?

Bugün darboğaz:

GPU sayısı değil.

GPU'ların birbirleriyle haberleşmesi.

Rakipleri Kimler?

En büyük rakipler:

Lumentum Holdings $LITE 

Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI 

Corning $GLW 

Broadcom $AVGO 

Marvell Technology $MRVL

Ama COHR'nin farkı:

Lazer + Transceiver + Silicon Photonics + CPO bileşenlerini aynı çatı altında sunabilmesi.

Coherent Corp. Coherent, dünyanın en büyük fotonik (ışık teknolojileri) şirketlerinden biridir. Basit anlatırsak: NVDA hesap yapıyor.✅ MU veriyi depoluyor.✅ COHR ise bu verinin veri merkezinde ışıkla taşınmasını sağlıyor. ✅ Yani yapay zekanın sinir sistemi diyebileceğimiz katmanda yer alıyor. (İşte en can alıcı nokta bu ) Şirketin Ana İş Kolları : 1. Optik Transceiver'lar Bunlar veri merkezlerinde kullanılan özel modüller. Görevleri: GPU → GPU Sunucu → Sunucu Rack → Rack arasındaki veriyi ışıkla taşımak. AI modelleri büyüdükçe bu ürünlerin önemi katlanıyor. ⚡️⚡️ 2. Silicon Photonics Bence şirketin en kritik alanı. Elektrik sinyallerini ışığa dönüştürüyor. Avantajları: Daha az enerji tüketimi✅ Daha yüksek hız✅ Daha düşük gecikme✅ Daha az ısı✅ NVIDIA'nın gelecek nesil veri merkezi mimarisinde tam olarak ihtiyaç duyulan teknoloji bu. 3. Lazerler COHR dünyanın en büyük endüstriyel ve telekom lazer üreticilerinden biri. Ürettikleri: InP (Indium Phosphide) lazerler CW lazerler VCSEL lazerler Telekom lazerleri Bu lazerler hem kendi ürünlerinde hem de sektörün diğer oyuncularında kullanılıyor. 4. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) Şu anda Wall Street'in en heyecanlandığı konu. Bugün: GPU ↔ Optik modül ayrı. Yarın: GPU ↔ Optik modül aynı pakette olacak. Bu teknolojiye CPO deniyor. NVIDIA'nın gelecekteki veri merkezi planlarının merkezinde bu teknoloji var. COHR da burada ana tedarikçilerden biri olmaya çalışıyor. Kimlerle Çalışıyor? Kesin müşteri listeleri her zaman açıklanmıyor ancak sektör analizlerinde ve şirket sunumlarında şu isimler sürekli karşımıza çıkıyor: NVIDIA Microsoft Azure Amazon Web Services Meta Google Cloud Çünkü bunların hepsi devasa AI veri merkezleri kuruyor ve optik ağ altyapısına ihtiyaç duyuyorlar. NVIDIA ile İlişkisi Burası çok önemli. 2026'da $NVDA : Coherent'e yaklaşık 2 milyar dolar yatırım yaptı. Uzun vadeli satın alma anlaşmaları yaptı. Lazer ve optik bileşen kapasitesini garanti altına aldı. Bu haber çıktığında COHR hissesi sert yükselmişti. Çünkü bu durum: "NVIDIA gelecekte fotoniğin kritik olacağını düşünüyor." mesajını verdi. Neden Yapay Zeka İçin Kritik? Bugün darboğaz: GPU sayısı değil. GPU'ların birbirleriyle haberleşmesi. Rakipleri Kimler? En büyük rakipler: Lumentum Holdings $LITE Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI Corning $GLW Broadcom $AVGO Marvell Technology $MRVL Ama COHR'nin farkı: Lazer + Transceiver + Silicon Photonics + CPO bileşenlerini aynı çatı altında sunabilmesi."}}}, "rest_id": "2068380860089553357", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1125", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
This week I learned the photonics trade is finally shifting from vibes to contracts.

$AXTI got a RMB173M / USD25.4M InP wafer deal with prepay/minimum-buy language. IQE got Tower commitments. AEHR got a follow-on SiPh test order.

Still tiny-scout territory. Proof is not size.
Go $GLW guys if you want to participate in the Photonics scale-up 😉

text:
The 5 best stocks and ETFs to own right now: 👇

$DRAM - memory ETF that includes $SNDK, $MU, Samsung and more. This is a growing space and will only continue to grow.

$TSLA - high and volatile gas prices are increasing demand for EVs. Energy storage systems, charging stations and autonomous capabilities are growing immensely. Robots are coming soon.

$SOFI - with oil stabilizing over time, the chances for rate cuts due to lower inflation is higher. This company is highly profitable and is scaling up quickly. They’re projecting 40% growth over the next 3-4 years.

$AAOI - photonics is a huge market and is growing in demand as AI data centers scale. These guys are positioned well to crush it over the next 5+ years. 

$ONDS - there has been a huge increase in the demand of autonomous drones, boats and on land vehicles used in military operations across the globe. Ondas has all the right products and services the U.S. military and other allied nations would need. 

What 5 stocks would you choose? 🤔

text:
$ALMU is one of the most exciting companies I’ve come across because it sits at the intersection of AI, photonics, semiconductors, defense, and space.

While the market is focused on GPUs and data centers, Aeluma is focused on one of the biggest challenges of the next decade:

💡 Moving data faster.
⚡ Using less power.
🔗 Eliminating connectivity bottlenecks.

The company is developing advanced photonic and semiconductor technologies that combine high-performance materials with scalable silicon manufacturing targeting AI infrastructure, optical interconnects, LiDAR, aerospace, defense, and next-generation communications.

What makes the story interesting isn’t just the technology.

It’s the validation.

🏆 Aeluma has secured contracts and development programs supported by NASA and the U.S. Department of Defense.

🏆 The company has participated in programs advancing technologies for space, aerospace, quantum sensing, and next-generation communications.

🏆 Aeluma has announced collaborations with organizations including Tower Semiconductor and AIM Photonics, helping position its technology within established semiconductor ecosystems.

As AI scales, the challenge isn’t simply creating more compute.

It’s moving enormous amounts of data between chips, servers, racks, and data centers efficiently.

That’s where photonics comes in.

And that’s where Aeluma is trying to build its advantage.

The reason I own $ALMU isn’t because it’s the biggest company.

It’s because it’s attacking a critical bottleneck.

The future of AI won’t just be determined by who builds the fastest processors.

It will also be determined by who can move information faster, cheaper, and more efficiently.

Power.
Compute.
Connectivity.

Aeluma sits directly in the connectivity layer.

🌡️MARKET CONDITIONS
- Overall mood is strongly BULLISH — he says markets are now trading at fresh all-time highs and dips should be treated as opportunities ("make dips your friend, and bears your enemy").

- He flagged the recent pullback as a Nasdaq "bear trap" (a fake-looking drop that traps sellers) which bottomed on June 9.

- Breadth looks healthy: he pointed to roughly 50 stocks showing relative strength (outperforming the broader market).

- He referenced the VIX (the market's "fear gauge") and the "rule of 16" to estimate how big daily index swings could be based on the VIX level.

- He warns the current FOMO (fear of missing out) is "obvious" and suggests waiting for a few-percent pullback before chasing stocks.

- Hot leadership themes he's watching: Quantum, Semiconductors, AI, Software, Bio/Health, Memory, and Data Centers.

📌KEY TAKEAWAYS
- He is HOLDING both $DRAM and $MRAM and frames new-high stocks as likely to keep climbing.

- He sees memory/storage and semis ($SNDK, $MU, $WDC, $STX, $INTC, $DELL, $AMD) as the year's standout winners.

- His method for catching big moves: on a monthly chart, find a base or trendline touch, then enter on the breakout (how he flagged $RDDT at 140).

📚GLOSSARY
- AWAP / VWAP-style average:
The average price a stock has traded at since listing; buyers often defend this level, making it useful support.

- Horizontal support:
A flat price floor where a stock has repeatedly stopped falling; losing it can trigger a sharp drop.

- Bear trap:
A misleading price drop that fools traders into selling or shorting right before the market reverses higher.

- Relative strength:
When a stock holds up or rises more than the overall market, a sign of leadership and demand.

- VIX (rule of 16):
The market's volatility/"fear" gauge; dividing it by 16 estimates the expected daily percentage move in the S&P 500.

- 52-week high:
The highest price a stock has hit in the past year; fresh highs often signal strong momentum.

- Monthly base / breakout:
A long sideways pause on the monthly chart, followed by a push above resistance that can start a big trend.

- YTD (Year-to-Date):
The performance of a stock counted from January 1 of the current year up to today.

text:
What do LED lighting, laser systems, modern displays, and solar panels have in common?

They all rely on the science of light.

In LEDs, Lasers and Sunlight, Miklós Lambert explores the technologies behind modern photonics, from LEDs and OLED displays to lasers, infrared systems, and solar energy. With more than 850 pages, this new book combines theory with practical engineering applications.

Whether you're working with lighting, optoelectronics, displays, or renewable energy, this guide offers a deep dive into one of the most important technologies shaping today's world.

Learn more:
https://t.co/dU3PZuS6nN

text:
$NOK 

Great buying point here IMO if you didn’t want to chase it at $16+. 

Lots of catalysts, in a crucial spot for Physical and edge AI (AI-RAN) with all the right collaborations behind it. A lot of people have forgotten the management hinting at a big milestone during the last ER (potentially Ai-RAN related?!) later this year that could prop up the stock.  Also photonics optionality after the recent acquisition of vertically-integrated Infinera that should develop throughout the year. 

Combined with the insider purchases at ATH, the glowing Nvidia investment, see no reason for the stock not to be double current price a year from now, especially as they shed portfolio/legacy segments. 

If you follow me i love businesses that make ‘pivots’, by shedding legacy low-margin businesses and becoming pure plays, making valuable acquisition, turning to a new higher-demand market, etc. It tends to make the story a lot clearer for institutions, and makes the stock very easy to rerate.

With all of this being said,  i am not a personal owner of $NOK, but sometimes the right way to underwrite a theme is to believe in multiple companies across the same ecosystem.

My thesis is that as Physical AI develops, edge AI, lower latency, and smarter network infrastructure become dramatically more important. 

Nokia is very well positioned be a dominant player here, given its NVIDIA partnership and domestic advantages. But where I see the highest upside is $HLIT (at ~2B MC vs $NOK at ~70B) as a more of an indirect beneficiary. It’s 95% monopoly over vCMTS gives it meaningful leverage if telecom, broadband, and network modernization become much more important in an AI-driven world. 

Remember my earlier post: if a provider like Comcast builds out edge capabilities across its network over the next decade to support large AI workloads across millions of residential homes, I’d presume the company selling the nodes Comcast depends on to enable those edge capabilities, as well as the integrated software that dramatically improves latency, would stand to benefit.

Just my musing and market thoughts. Have a great day

text:
🚨RECAP: @asklivermore 's posts in the last 24h. (June 17, 9:00AM ET)

📈STOCKS MENTIONED
- $CRWV (CoreWeave) — Holding / Bullish: Banks (BoA, Wells Fargo, BNP Paribas) reiterated Overweight with $150 price targets after a strong Q2 backlog beat. Needs to break above its pivot high (the recent swing high that acts as a ceiling) to run higher.

- $IREN (Iris Energy) — Holding: Kept as a core AI name in the portfolio.

- $V (Visa) — Buying: Added as part of overweighting financials.

- $MA (Mastercard) — Buying: Added alongside Visa to strengthen the financials side of the portfolio.

- $GEV (GE Vernova) — Holding / Overweight: Portfolio is leaning heavily into power/energy infrastructure names.

- $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) — Watching: Optical/photonics name consolidating (pausing) after a big run; keep on watchlist.

- $LITE (Lumentum) — Watching: Optical/photonics name resting after a strong move, building for a potential next leg.

- $COHR (Coherent) — Watching: Consolidating with the rest of the photonics group; trend still intact.

- $FN (Fabrinet) — Watching: Photonics name digesting gains; flagged as a "trends stay trends" candidate.

- $RKLB (Rocket Lab) — Buying on dips: Core space play backed by defense and Space Force spending.

- $ASTS (AST SpaceMobile) — Buying on dips: Space/satellite broadband name seen as a long-term national security beneficiary.

- $RDW (Redwire) — Buying on dips: Space components/infrastructure name to accumulate on weakness.

- $PL (Planet Labs) — Buying on dips: Satellite data name tied to defense, climate and AI training data demand.

- $SPCE, $LUNR, $SIDU, $SATL, $FLY, $BKSY, $SPIR — Watching: Additional space names highlighted as part of the broader space theme.

- $UFO, $NASA (Space ETFs) — Watching: Mentioned as basket ways to play the space sector.

- $NVDA (Nvidia) — Holding / Liking: Strong chart structure; a leader to help drag markets toward all-time highs.

- $AMZN (Amazon) — Holding / Liking: Mag 7 name with a structure he likes.

- $GOOGL (Alphabet) — Holding / Liking: Mag 7 name supporting the bullish market case.

- $AAPL (Apple) — Holding / Liking: Mag 7 name; healthy structure noted.

- $SPY (S&P 500 ETF) — Watching / Bullish: Has started to uptrend again with a breakout above a key level.

🌡️MARKET CONDITIONS
- Overall mood is BULLISH — he is "STILL leaning bullish" and puts a roughly 70% chance on the market reaching new all-time highs.

- The S&P 500 (tracked via $SPY) has started a fresh uptrend and is breaking out above a key pivot, which he sees as the main fuel for the move higher.

- Mega-cap tech (the "Mag 7" like Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet and Apple) showing healthy structure is the key condition needed to keep the rally going.

- Broad theme: AI infrastructure (CoreWeave, photonics names) and power/energy demand remain strong long-term drivers.

- Space is framed as a durable, government-backed theme tied to national security, so dips are treated as buying chances rather than warnings.

- His portfolio is built to stay "STRONG on red days and OUTPERFORM on green days," signaling a defensive-but-offensive posture rather than fear.

📌KEY TAKEAWAYS
- He is explicitly BULLISH on the overall market and expects a push toward all-time highs as long as the big tech leaders stay strong.

- He is BUYING financials (Visa, Mastercard) and staying overweight power (GE Vernova) and core AI names (CoreWeave, Iris Energy).

- He treats every DIP in space stocks (Rocket Lab, AST SpaceMobile, Redwire, Planet Labs) as a BUYING opportunity, not a reason to sell.

- Photonics/optical names (AAOI, Lumentum, Coherent, Fabrinet) are on WATCH — consolidating after big runs, so he's waiting rather than chasing.

- For CoreWeave specifically, the actionable trigger is a break above the pivot high; Wall Street's $150 targets give a longer-term upside reference.

- His mindset: "trends stay trends for a long time," meaning he prefers riding established uptrends and accumulating leaders on weakness.

📚GLOSSARY
- Pivot high:
The most recent swing high on a chart that acts like a ceiling; breaking above it often signals more upside.

- Overweight:
A rating (here from banks) meaning they expect a stock to outperform the market and suggest owning more of it.

- Consolidating:
When a stock pauses and trades sideways after a big move, "resting" before a possible next leg.

- Uptrend:
A pattern of higher highs and higher lows showing price is generally moving up over time.

- Breakout:
When price pushes above a key resistance level, often sparking a fresh move higher.

- ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue):
The yearly revenue a company expects from ongoing/subscription contracts; a key health metric for tech firms.

- Mag 7:
Shorthand for the seven mega-cap tech leaders (e.g. Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet, Apple) that heavily influence the market.

- Watchlist:
A list of stocks a trader monitors for a future entry rather than buying right away.

⚠️DISCLAIMER
We are not affiliated with @asklivermore in any way.

This summary is based on posts publicly shared over the past 24h and is provided for informational purposes only.

text:
AI infra is moving from hype to bottlenecks

bandwidth
power loss
specialized manufacturing

$AAOI helps datacenters move more data with 800g / 1.6t optics

$SOI makes photonics-soi substrates, materials that help chips communicate faster using light

$XFAB makes specialty power chips, including SiC and GaN, used to handle electricity more efficiently

plus, X-FAB is european

that matters when the west is trying to rebuild semiconductor sovereignty

these are the layers that help AI scale beyond the gpu

text:
10/12

This is also why people are talking about VCSEL, EML, and Silicon Photonics.

At 100G/lane, many architectures can still work well.

At 200G/lane, the trade-offs become much harder.

VCSEL has short-reach cost advantages.

EML may work better for high-speed SMF links.

SiPh + external laser may matter more for dense, low-power optical engines.

The number of Trio-tech positions available in Batu Kawan seems to be ~33, which is a significant workforce. More importantly, one of the adverts for the location specifically states that the "role will be based at MMP." https://t.co/xN8tf5FWZa

MMP at Batu Kawan stands for Micron Memory Penang. In job postings, internal references, LinkedIn posts, and university visits, Micron Memory Malaysia is routinely abbreviated as MMP. https://t.co/tHQ8xSb6ld

This puts TRT into the supply chain of another critical theme, on top of the AI/GPU ($10m in orders since March 2026), photonics (see my previous post on $CORH and $TRT), and now memory. 

Microns Malaysia's facility focuses on semiconductor/memory manufacturing. Specifically, assembly, test, burn-in, and related processes for DRAM, NAND, and SSD products. text:
The market rewards those who identify important themes early.

These are the themes I spend the most time researching.

Bookmark this.  

• Photonics → $AAOI $MRVL $AEHR $LITE
• Space → $SPCX $RKLB $ASTS $LUNR $PL 
• AI Infra → $NBIS $CFIR $IREN
• Energy → $CEG $VST $OKLO $SMR
• Power Semis → $VICR $NVTS $MPWR $ON
• AI Inference → $AMD $ARM $INTC 
• Robotics → $TSLA $VPG $AMBA $OUST
• Quantum → $IONQ $INFQ $RGTI
• Rare Earths → $MP $UUUU $USAR $CRML 

The goal isn't to predict every winner.

It's to understand where the world is investing for the future.
